E-Com Plus.description: E-Com Plus is an open fair-code ecommerce platform designed to be used with headless commerce APIs.
E-Com Plus.history: E-Com Plus is a Brazilian open-source headless e-commerce platform that combines SaaS convenience with open-source freedom. The platform provides cloud-native APIs for digital commerce targeted at Latin American merchants, with an Astro and Vue storefront, built-in CMS, and integrations for payments, shipping, ERPs, and CRMs. The project is available on GitHub under the ecomplus organization.
E-Com Plus.summary_abuse: E-Com Plus is a legitimate open-source e-commerce platform focused on Latin America with no known abuse incidents. As an open-source project, the code is publicly auditable. It is a niche platform with limited enterprise adoption outside Brazil.

e-goi.description: e-goi is a multichannel marketing automation software for ecommerce.
e-goi.history: e-goi is a multichannel marketing automation platform founded around 2005 in Porto, Portugal. It serves primarily Spanish and Portuguese-speaking markets and offers email, SMS, voice, and web push marketing capabilities.
e-goi.summary_abuse: e-goi is a marketing automation platform with email, SMS, and push notification capabilities. Like all bulk messaging platforms, it carries a risk of being used for spam campaigns. The platform applies anti-abuse controls, but marketing automation tools are a common vector for unsolicited commercial messaging.

E-monsite.description: E-monsite is a web-based platform that allows users to create and customise their own websites using a range of templates and features, without requiring coding or technical skills.
E-monsite.history: E-monsite was created in 2004 by Arnaud Jibaut and is headquartered in Amiens, France. It is a freemium website building platform that allows anyone to create a website without technical knowledge, supporting personal sites, business pages, and e-commerce stores. The platform is particularly popular in France and French-speaking countries, offering multilingual support through its emyspot international version.
E-monsite.summary_abuse: E-monsite is a legitimate French website builder with no known abuse incidents. The company has operated for over 20 years and is GDPR-compliant. It is primarily used by non-technical French-speaking individuals and small businesses. No security incidents or malicious behavior have been reported.

e-Shop Commerce.description: e-Shop is a all-in-one Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) that allows Israeli customers to set up an online store and sell their products.
e-Shop Commerce.history: e-Shop Commerce is an Israeli all-in-one e-commerce SaaS platform for setting up online stores. Limited founding details are publicly available.
e-Shop Commerce.summary_abuse: e-Shop Commerce is a hosted e-commerce platform. Like other store builders, it could potentially host fraudulent storefronts, but no specific documented incidents are known. Its focus on Israeli market operators limits broader abuse exposure.

e107.history: e107 is an open-source PHP/MySQL CMS first launched in July 2002 by a developer known as jalist. It went through multiple major versions, with version 2.0 being a complete rewrite released officially in 2015 that modernized the codebase to use Bootstrap and contemporary PHP practices.
e107.summary_abuse: e107 is a self-hosted open-source CMS with no notable abuse signal as a platform itself. Like any self-hosted CMS it can be installed on compromised or malicious hosting, but the project has no consumer-facing SaaS surface for scammers to exploit directly.

E37.description: E37 is an enterprise ecommerce platform designed to support large-scale online retail operations.
E37.history: E37 is a Swedish enterprise e-commerce platform marketed as 'Smart e-handel' (Smart e-commerce). The platform powers approximately 400 online stores and is designed for large-scale enterprise retail operations. The platform has experienced a slight decline in usage over recent quarters. It is used primarily in Scandinavia.
E37.summary_abuse: E37 is a legitimate Swedish enterprise e-commerce platform with no known abuse incidents. The platform hosts a limited number of stores and is aimed at established retailers rather than anonymous or low-barrier users. No security incidents or malicious behavior have been reported.

EAD Plataforma.description: EAD Plataforma is a distance learning platform designed to deliver online educational content and support remote instruction across various subjects.
EAD Plataforma.history: EAD Plataforma is a Brazilian e-learning SaaS platform that enables educational institutions and content creators to build and sell online courses. The platform has worked with over 1,000 customers worldwide, creating 1,900+ branded platforms. It partners with video security providers like VdoCipher for DRM-protected video content delivery.
EAD Plataforma.summary_abuse: EAD Plataforma is a legitimate Brazilian online learning platform with no known abuse incidents. The platform is used by educators and institutions to sell online courses. It targets Brazilian Portuguese-speaking markets with no notable abuse or security incidents reported.

Eaglecart.description: Eaglecart is a platform that consolidates online store operations, point-of-sale systems, and inventory management across multiple regions, currencies, and warehouse locations.
Eaglecart.history: Eaglecart is a newer all-in-one e-commerce platform that unifies online store, POS, and inventory management across regions, currencies, and warehouses. The platform offers no-code website building, multi-channel sales via TikTok and Instagram, WhatsApp customer management, and enterprise logistics with barcode scanning. It offers a 14-day free trial with zero transaction fees.
Eaglecart.summary_abuse: Eaglecart is a legitimate e-commerce platform with no known abuse incidents. It is a newer entrant with limited public track record and no notable security certifications. As an e-commerce platform with free trial access, it carries standard exposure to fraudulent storefront creation.

EagleView.description: EagleView is a provider of GIS mapping, aerial imagery tools, and analytics used to deliver accurate geospatial data.
EagleView.history: EagleView Technologies was founded in 2008 by Chris Pershing and Dave Carlson to provide aerial imagery and property data analytics for insurance, construction, and government sectors. In 2013 the company merged with aerial imagery provider Pictometry International. EagleView was acquired by Vista Equity Partners in 2015 and received additional investment from Clearlake Capital in 2018. In 2025 it launched EagleView One, a comprehensive geospatial intelligence platform.
EagleView.summary_abuse: EagleView is a legitimate geospatial intelligence and aerial imagery platform serving insurance, construction, and government clients with no known abuse incidents. The platform collects high-resolution aerial imagery of properties, raising privacy considerations about surveillance, but operates within regulatory frameworks for government and commercial use.

EarlyParrot.description: EarlyParrot is a referral marketing platform that supports coaches, course creators, and event hosts in expanding launches, webinars, and email lists through effective referral strategies.
EarlyParrot.history: EarlyParrot is a referral marketing platform founded around 2019, headquartered in Malta. The platform allows SaaS products, e-commerce stores, and content creators to add referral programs by embedding JavaScript tags. It supports sharing via email, social media, WhatsApp, and unique referral URLs, and provides reward and milestone tracking for referrers.
EarlyParrot.summary_abuse: EarlyParrot is a legitimate referral marketing SaaS with no known abuse incidents. The platform is used by small businesses to incentivize referrals. No security incidents or fraudulent use have been publicly reported.

EaselJS.description: EaselJS is a JavaScript library designed to simplify working with the HTML5 Canvas element, making it useful for creating games, generative art, and other graphical applications.
EaselJS.history: EaselJS is a JavaScript library created by gskinner.com for working with HTML5 Canvas elements, providing a hierarchical display list and interactive model for building games, ads, and data visualizations. It is part of the CreateJS suite alongside SoundJS, PreloadJS, and TweenJS. The library is released under the MIT license and has been available since around 2011.
EaselJS.summary_abuse: EaselJS is a safe MIT-licensed open-source library with no known abuse incidents. It is used by developers to create canvas-based web experiences and has no consumer-facing surface that would enable abuse.

EasiChat.description: Easichat is an online customer service platform that uses AI to automate and manage customer interactions.
EasiChat.history: EasiChat is a UK-based SaaS company providing AI-powered live chat software that blends AI chatbots with human agents, targeting SMEs, call centres, and customer service bureaus in the United Kingdom. The platform allows one agent to manage up to ten simultaneous conversations by using AI to suggest responses while allowing human override. It integrates across websites, social media, and messaging apps.
EasiChat.summary_abuse: EasiChat is a legitimate UK live chat platform with no known abuse incidents. The company is GDPR-compliant and targets UK businesses. No security incidents or fraudulent use have been publicly reported.

Easol.description: Easol is a platform that enables businesses to create and manage their experience-based operations.
Easol.history: Easol was founded in 2017 by Ben Simpson and Lisa Simpson and is headquartered in London, UK. The company describes itself as the Shopify for experience businesses and has raised £19 million in Series A funding, one of the largest Series A raises for a UK SaaS company. The platform enables festivals, wellness retreats, food and drink experiences, sports and adventure trips to manage bookings, payments, and websites in one place.
Easol.summary_abuse: Easol is a legitimate experience commerce platform with no known abuse incidents. The company is VC-backed and GDPR-compliant, operating in the regulated UK market. No security incidents or fraudulent use have been publicly reported.

Easy Accordion.description: Easy Accordion is a flexible and customisable accordion and FAQs builder plugin for WordPress.
Easy Accordion.history: Easy Accordion is a WordPress plugin for creating responsive accordion and FAQ sections using a drag-and-drop interface. Available on WordPress.org as a free plugin with a Pro paid version offering additional features including analytics, AJAX search, and more animation options. The plugin integrates with Elementor, Gutenberg, Divi, and other page builders.
Easy Accordion.summary_abuse: Easy Accordion is a legitimate WordPress plugin with no known abuse incidents. It is GPL-licensed, actively maintained, and used for creating accordion and FAQ UI components. No security vulnerabilities or malicious behavior have been publicly reported.

Easy Hide PayPal.description: Easy Hide PayPal hides PayPal button from product page, cart and checkout but keep PayPal as payment option in checkout.
Easy Hide PayPal.history: Easy Hide PayPal is a Shopify app that hides the PayPal button from product pages, cart pages, and checkout while still keeping PayPal as a payment option in the checkout flow. The app targets Shopify merchants who prefer to show alternative payment methods more prominently. It is a lightweight single-purpose Shopify app.
Easy Hide PayPal.summary_abuse: Easy Hide PayPal is a legitimate Shopify app with no known abuse incidents. It is a narrow single-purpose app for payment UI customization. No security incidents or fraudulent use have been reported.

Easy Orders.description: Easy Orders is an ecommerce platform that offers a pricing plan where users can create their online store and pay a fee of 0.5 EGP per order.
Easy Orders.history: Easy Orders is an Egyptian e-commerce platform that offers per-order pricing at 0.5 EGP per order, targeting small businesses in Egypt and the MENA region. The platform allows users to create online stores with a usage-based pricing model rather than flat subscription fees.
Easy Orders.summary_abuse: Easy Orders is a legitimate Egyptian e-commerce platform with no known abuse incidents. Limited public information is available on security practices and compliance. The platform is a niche product targeting Egyptian small businesses.

Easy Pie Chart.description: Lightweight jQuery plugin to render and animate nice pie charts with the HTML5 canvas element.
Easy Pie Chart.history: Easy Pie Chart is a lightweight JavaScript plugin created by Robert Fleischmann (rendro) for drawing animated, customizable pie and ring charts using the HTML5 Canvas element. Released under the MIT license around 2012, the library is available as a jQuery plugin and as a vanilla JavaScript version. The project is available on cdnjs and npm.
Easy Pie Chart.summary_abuse: Easy Pie Chart is a safe MIT-licensed open-source chart rendering library with no known abuse incidents. The project appears dormant with no recent active maintenance, but remains functional. There are no known security vulnerabilities or malicious behavior.

Easy Redirects.description: Easy Redirects is a Shopify app built by Eastside, and part of the best Shopify Apps collection.
Easy Redirects.history: Easy Redirects was created by Eastside Co, a UK-based Shopify agency, as a Shopify app for managing 301 redirects and fixing 404 errors. The app was later acquired by Shop Circle, a Shopify app portfolio company that aggregates and scales Shopify apps, and rebranded as SC Easy Redirects.
Easy Redirects.summary_abuse: Easy Redirects is a Shopify redirect management app with no notable abuse signal. Its function is limited to URL redirect management within the Shopify ecosystem, which poses minimal abuse risk. The app requires Shopify merchant authentication to install, preventing anonymous usage.

Easy Rez.description: Easy Rez is a hotel property management and booking system designed to streamline reservations, room management, and guest services.
Easy Rez.history: Easy Rez was founded in 2010 as a hotel property management and booking system. Limited additional public history is available.
Easy Rez.summary_abuse: Easy Rez is a small hotel management and booking system with no notable abuse signal.

Easy Web Editor.description: Easy Web Editor is a website builder platform that enables users to create and manage web pages through a visual interface without requiring advanced coding knowledge.
Easy Web Editor.history: Easy Web Editor is a visual website builder platform targeting non-technical users. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Easy Web Editor.summary_abuse: Easy Web Editor is a visual website builder with no documented abuse signal. As a hosting-adjacent platform, it could theoretically be used to host phishing pages, but no specific incidents are documented.

EasyBroker.description: EasyBroker is a property management platform facilitating sharing of properties through WhatsApp and email.
EasyBroker.history: EasyBroker was founded in 2007 by Eric Northam in Mexico City as a CRM and MLS platform for real estate professionals in Mexico and Latin America. It has grown to become one of the most widely used real estate software platforms in the Mexican market, offering lead management, property listing syndication, and website creation for agents and agencies.
EasyBroker.summary_abuse: EasyBroker is a legitimate real estate CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves real estate professionals with KYC-controlled access and is not a common vector for fraud or scams.

EasyDigitalDownloads.description: Easy Digital Downloads is a WordPress ecommerce plugin that focuses purely on digital products.
EasyDigitalDownloads.history: Easy Digital Downloads was created by Pippin Williamson and released in April 2012 by his company Sandhills Development. It became one of the most popular WordPress plugins for selling digital products, with over 50,000 active installs. Sandhills Development was later acquired by Awesome Motive, a major WordPress plugin holding company that also owns WPForms, MonsterInsights, and OptinMonster.
EasyDigitalDownloads.summary_abuse: Easy Digital Downloads is a widely used legitimate WordPress ecommerce plugin. As a self-hosted solution for selling digital files, it carries standard WordPress ecosystem risks including plugin vulnerability exploitation. There are no notable patterns of the plugin itself being used as an abuse vector, and the maintainer Awesome Motive has a responsible disclosure policy.

EasyEngine.description: EasyEngine is a command-line tool for the Nginx web servers to manage WordPress sites that are running on the LEMP Stack.
EasyEngine.history: EasyEngine was created by rtCamp, an India-based WordPress development agency, and first released in 2012 as a command-line tool to simplify WordPress deployment on Nginx servers. It grew to manage over 75,000 WordPress sites globally. In 2023, rtCamp launched a web dashboard for EasyEngine alongside WP Cloud integration, expanding beyond CLI-only management. The CLI remains MIT-licensed open source.
EasyEngine.summary_abuse: EasyEngine is a server management tool used by legitimate WordPress agencies and developers. As a CLI tool and dashboard for managing server infrastructure, it has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface. Abuse risk is minimal and limited to standard server management misuse scenarios.

EasyGift.description: EasyGift is a tool that automates the addition of free gifts or products to the cart based on custom rules, allowing for upsells, BOGO (Buy 1 Get 1) offers, and the creation of rules based on cart value or specific products, with the ability to schedule start times for offer activation.
EasyGift.history: EasyGift is a Shopify app originally developed by FiftyPoint6 to automate free gift and product add-on offers based on cart rules. The app was later acquired by Shop Circle, a Shopify app portfolio company, and rebranded as EG Auto Add to Cart Free Gift.
EasyGift.summary_abuse: EasyGift is a Shopify cart automation app with no notable abuse signal. The app requires Shopify merchant authentication and is limited to gift-with-purchase automation within the Shopify ecosystem.

EasyLiao.description: EasyLiao is a pre-sales customer agent system developed in China, designed to support businesses in handling customer inquiries and engagement before a sale.
EasyLiao.history: EasyLiao is a China-based chatbot and conversational AI platform primarily serving businesses in Southeast Asia. The company has approximately 10 employees and revenues around $1 million. No major ownership changes or notable events are documented.
EasyLiao.summary_abuse: EasyLiao is a small China-based chatbot platform with limited public presence. No notable abuse cases are documented. As a conversational AI tool serving businesses, it has limited direct consumer-facing abuse risk.

Easyling.description: Easyling is a website translation solution that provides tools for localizing websites through multiple methods.
Easyling.history: Easyling is a translation proxy platform developed by a Hungarian company to enable CMS-agnostic website localization without code changes. The platform works as a reverse proxy, translating content on-the-fly using translation memories and integrating with professional translators and CAT tools.
Easyling.summary_abuse: Easyling is a translation proxy service with no notable abuse signal. As a localization technology used by businesses for website translation, it does not expose common abuse vectors such as anonymous hosting or mass messaging.

Easylog.description: EasyLog is a logistics company based in Brazil.
Easylog.history: easyLog Limited was formed in 2004 following a management buyout from Feedback plc, a London Stock Exchange-listed company, and is based in Kent, UK. The company provides cloud-hosted warehouse management and staff attendance software serving both private and public sector clients ranging from multinationals to small owner-managed companies.
Easylog.summary_abuse: Easylog is a UK-based warehouse management and logistics SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves B2B supply chain clients and does not expose consumer-facing abuse risk vectors.

EasyPolls.description: EasyPolls is a tool that allows poll creation and sharing across websites, Facebook, Twitter, or via a direct link to the poll, enabling users to engage audiences across multiple platforms.
EasyPolls.history: EasyPolls is an online polling tool created by ObjectPlanet, a Norwegian software company. It provides embeddable polls with sharing capabilities for websites, social media, and newsletters. The service has been operating for over a decade as a free-tier polling solution with paid upgrades for advanced features.
EasyPolls.summary_abuse: EasyPolls is a polling tool with minimal documented abuse. Its free tier could theoretically be used to create manipulative polls, but its limited reach and lack of anonymous hosting features reduce abuse risk. ObjectPlanet has standard terms of service covering prohibited uses.

Easysize.description: Easysize is a system that provides clothing size recommendations to enhance fit and reduce sizing errors.
Easysize.history: Easysize is an AI-powered clothing size recommendation platform founded to reduce online fashion returns by helping shoppers identify the right size before purchase. The tool integrates with Shopify and other ecommerce platforms, using questionnaire-based sizing logic rather than body measurements.
Easysize.summary_abuse: Easysize is a fashion sizing recommendation tool with no notable abuse signal. The tool collects sizing preferences but does not host user content or provide services that are commonly exploited for fraud.

EasyStore.description: EasyStore is a multi sales channel ecommerce platform.
EasyStore.history: EasyStore was founded in 2012 by Frost Chen and Alan Kok in Puchong, Malaysia. It has grown to become one of Southeast Asia's leading hosted ecommerce platforms, serving over 50,000 brands. The platform focuses on providing multi-channel selling capabilities with native support for regional payment gateways and logistics providers in Malaysia and Southeast Asia.
EasyStore.summary_abuse: EasyStore is a legitimate ecommerce platform serving small and medium businesses in Southeast Asia. As an ecommerce platform, it could theoretically be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but the company has standard KYC and terms of service. No notable abuse patterns have been documented.

EasyUI.description: EasyUI is a UI framework that provides a set of customizable components and widgets for building interactive web applications.
EasyUI.history: EasyUI (jEasyUI) is a jQuery-based UI component library for building enterprise web interfaces. It provides data grids, forms, dialogs, and layout components. The library has been available for over a decade with a dual-license model offering a free community edition and a commercial license for enterprise use.
EasyUI.summary_abuse: EasyUI is a frontend JavaScript UI library with no meaningful abuse signal. As a component library embedded in enterprise web applications, it has no consumer-facing attack surface that would make it a target for abuse.

EasyWebinar.description: EasyWebinar is a webinar platform designed for marketing and growing businesses.
EasyWebinar.history: EasyWebinar is a San Francisco-based webinar platform supporting both live and automated webinar hosting. The platform has been active since around 2013 and caters to marketing, sales, and educational use cases, with features for real-time interaction and evergreen on-demand replays.
EasyWebinar.summary_abuse: EasyWebinar is a legitimate webinar platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform requires registration and payment, limiting anonymous misuse. Standard webinar platforms have minimal documented abuse risk.

EasyWeek.description: EasyWeek is a software platform that enables appointment scheduling for businesses across various industries.
EasyWeek.history: EasyWeek is an online appointment booking and scheduling platform targeting beauty salons, barbershops, healthcare centers, and other service businesses. The platform integrates with Google Business, social media, and multiple messaging channels for booking notifications. It offers a free tier and has expanded internationally.
EasyWeek.summary_abuse: EasyWeek is an appointment scheduling platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves legitimate service businesses and does not expose features commonly exploited for fraud or spam.

Eatself.description: Eatself is an online platform that facilitates meal ordering and delivery services.
Eatself.history: Eatself is a niche online food ordering and delivery platform. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Eatself.summary_abuse: Eatself is a food ordering and delivery SaaS with no documented abuse signal. Its use case is legitimate restaurant management and no features commonly targeted for fraud are documented.

EatStreet.description: EatStreet is a food booking platform that enables users to browse restaurants, place meal orders, and schedule deliveries or pickups through an online or mobile interface.
EatStreet.history: EatStreet was founded in 2010 in Madison, Wisconsin as an online food ordering marketplace connecting diners with local restaurants. It grew to cover over 200 US markets with over 15,000 restaurant partners, focusing on mid-sized cities underserved by larger competitors. In October 2024, EatStreet filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, signaling the end of its independent operation.
EatStreet.summary_abuse: EatStreet is a food delivery marketplace with no notable direct abuse pattern. As a consumer-facing platform with payment processing, it carries standard risks of account fraud and fraudulent orders. No significant documented abuse incidents are on record, though the company filed for bankruptcy in 2024.

Ebasnet.description: Ebasnet is a web project creation and management platform in the cloud. It allows anyone to set up an online store or corporate website without prior IT knowledge.
Ebasnet.history: Ebasnet is a European SaaS platform for creating online stores and corporate websites without coding knowledge. The platform hosts its infrastructure within European Union data centers with GDPR-compliant data handling. It offers a 30-day free trial and multilingual support.
Ebasnet.summary_abuse: Ebasnet is a small European website builder and ecommerce platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a no-code website builder, it could theoretically host spam storefronts, but its limited market presence reduces this risk.

eBay Partner Network.description: eBay Partner Network is an online referral program where eBay pays commissions to referrers on sales generated by customers they’ve referred.
eBay Partner Network.history: eBay Partner Network is the affiliate marketing program operated by eBay Inc., one of the world's largest e-commerce platforms. The program has been running since the early 2000s and went through a rebrand and technology overhaul around 2008-2013, including a partnership with Commission Junction (now CJ Affiliate) that was later brought in-house.
eBay Partner Network.summary_abuse: eBay Partner Network has moderate abuse exposure typical of affiliate programs: fraudulent clicks, cookie stuffing, and fake referrals are documented industry-wide issues. eBay has fraud detection in place and will suspend affiliates for policy violations. The program itself is not a preferred phishing or scam hosting platform.

eBay Store.description: eBay is a platform that either redirects users to eBay or displays a link to an eBay store.
eBay Store.history: eBay was founded in 1995 by Pierre Omidyar and is one of the world's largest e-commerce marketplaces. The eBay Store feature allows sellers to create branded storefronts within the eBay marketplace. eBay went public in 1998 and has undergone various acquisitions and divestitures including PayPal (spun off 2015).
eBay Store.summary_abuse: eBay Store and the broader eBay marketplace have well-documented fraud issues including counterfeit goods listings, scam seller accounts, feedback manipulation, and phishing emails impersonating eBay. eBay operates buyer protection programs and abuse reporting but the scale of the platform means fraud is ongoing. Security researchers regularly document eBay-themed phishing campaigns.

Eber.description: Eber is a consumer engagement management platform for retail, F&B, hotels, and more.
Eber.history: Eber is a Singapore-based loyalty marketing platform serving retail, F&B, beauty, and lifestyle businesses across Southeast Asia. The platform provides points-based loyalty programs, membership management, and marketing campaign automation with over 80 integrations. It is listed on Singapore's GoBusiness Productivity Solutions Grant.
Eber.summary_abuse: Eber is a loyalty marketing platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves legitimate businesses and handles customer data for loyalty programs, with standard data protection practices.

ebisumart.description: ebisumart is a cloud-based storefront system for developing and renewing high-quality ecommerce websites.
ebisumart.history: ebisumart is a cloud-based ecommerce platform developed by InterFactory, Inc. (TYO: 4057), a Tokyo-based company founded in 2003. The platform is described as Japan's #1 share cloud-based ecommerce system and is used for building and renewing high-quality ecommerce websites. ebisumart has also been made available as the storefront system of transcosmos's eCHUB commerce series, reflecting a key B2B2B partnership in Japan's ecommerce infrastructure market.
ebisumart.summary_abuse: ebisumart is a Japanese B2B ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves established merchants through managed contracts and is not freely available for anonymous or low-friction signup. No documented phishing, scam hosting, or fraud incidents are associated with ebisumart.

EC-CUBE.description: EC-CUBE is an open source package used to build ecommerce sites.
EC-CUBE.history: EC-CUBE is the leading e-commerce open-source platform in Japan, released in 2006. Since its release, users have been able to build e-commerce sites for free. The platform is built using PHP 8.x with the Symfony 6.4 framework and Doctrine ORM, and powers over 35,000 online stores primarily in Japan.
EC-CUBE.summary_abuse: EC-CUBE is a legitimate open-source e-commerce platform dominant in Japan with no inherent abuse issues, though like all e-commerce platforms it can be used to host fraudulent storefronts if misconfigured. The platform has known security vulnerabilities patched over the years, with regular security releases. Self-hosted deployments require operators to apply patches.

Ecal.description: Ecal is a marketing software platform that enables the delivery of calendar-based tickets and events directly into users’ personal calendars.
Ecal.history: ECAL is an Australian calendar marketing platform that pioneered sync-to-calendar technology, enabling brands to push event and schedule updates directly into subscribers' personal calendars. The platform is used by top sports, media, and ticketing organizations including Formula E, NBA teams, and Premier League clubs.
Ecal.summary_abuse: ECAL is a calendar marketing platform serving established sports and entertainment brands. No notable abuse signal. The platform does not expose anonymous content hosting or mass messaging features that are commonly exploited.

eCaupo.description: eCaupo is no delivery portal, but your own shop.
eCaupo.history: eCaupo is a niche ecommerce SaaS platform positioning itself as a direct online shop alternative to delivery portals, likely targeting the German-speaking market; no notable ownership changes found.
eCaupo.summary_abuse: eCaupo is a small ecommerce shop platform. No documented abuse signal or fraud patterns have been reported for this platform.

ECBB.description: ECBB is a Japan-based platform providing data analytics and business intelligence solutions.
ECBB.history: ECBB is a Japanese web development and e-commerce solutions company active since 1999, with experience in over 5,000 website and system development projects. The company provides e-commerce site construction, CMS development, proprietary affiliate systems, print distribution platforms, and mobile app development for iOS and Android. It serves Japanese businesses seeking comprehensive web IT solutions.
ECBB.summary_abuse: ECBB is a legitimate Japanese web development company with no known abuse incidents. The company focuses on building custom e-commerce and web solutions for Japanese businesses. Limited public information is available on security practices and certifications.

ecbeing.description: Ecbeing is a platform designed for building ecommerce sites, tailored for medium-sized and large companies.
ecbeing.history: ecbeing is a Japanese enterprise e-commerce platform specifically designed for medium-to-large companies; it is one of the established players in the Japanese enterprise e-commerce market.
ecbeing.summary_abuse: ecbeing is a Japanese enterprise e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; its enterprise-only pricing and Japan-focused market limit broad consumer fraud exposure.

EcForce.description: EcForce is an all-in-one ecommerce platform with all the functions necessary for ecommerce, from landing-page creation to order and customer data management analysis.
EcForce.history: EcForce is a Japan-based all-in-one ecommerce platform targeting D2C and subscription businesses. The platform has processed over 100 billion JPY in total sales and has around 424 live stores in Japan. It integrates with Japanese-domestic payment services and logistics providers, with a focus on high-functionality for larger merchants.
EcForce.summary_abuse: EcForce is a legitimate Japanese ecommerce platform with no notable documented abuse. As an ecommerce platform serving verified merchants, it has standard anti-fraud measures and no significant pattern of misuse.

eChalk.description: eChalk is a school website and learning management system.
eChalk.history: eChalk is a US-based school website and learning management system vendor serving K-12 institutions; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are publicly documented.
eChalk.summary_abuse: eChalk is a K-12 school website and LMS platform with no notable abuse signal; its institutional-only sales model limits exposure to public abuse.

ECharts.description: ECharts is an open-source JavaScript visualisation library.
ECharts.history: Apache ECharts was originally created by Baidu's data visualization team (called EFE) and open-sourced in 2013. It was donated to the Apache Software Foundation in 2018 and graduated as an Apache Top-Level Project in 2021. The library is written in JavaScript, uses ZRender as its graphics rendering engine, and supports Canvas and SVG rendering with over 20 chart types.
ECharts.summary_abuse: Apache ECharts is a foundational open-source data visualization library under the Apache Software Foundation with no known abuse incidents. It is used by developers to render charts in browsers and has no consumer-facing surface that would enable abuse.

eClass.description: eClass is an online learning platform.
eClass.history: eClass is an open-source learning management system developed by the Greek Academic Network GUnet, used primarily by Greek universities and educational institutions; it has been maintained since the early 2000s with no major ownership or deprecation events.
eClass.summary_abuse: eClass is a self-hosted academic LMS with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

EcomArtists.description: EcomArtists is a provider of custom drawn products and gifts, tailored for personalisation, ideal for sharing with loved ones.
EcomArtists.history: EcomArtists is a personalization and ecommerce platform offering custom-drawn products and gifts. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
EcomArtists.summary_abuse: EcomArtists is a niche personalized gifts ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signal.

EcomCart.description: EcomCart is a platform that enables the creation and management of an online store for selling products and services.
EcomCart.history: EcomCart is a European ecommerce platform (ecomcart.eu) for online store creation and management. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
EcomCart.summary_abuse: EcomCart is a niche ecommerce SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

eComchain.description: eComchain is a cloud-based ecommerce platform offering B2B and B2C solutions with integrated enterprise resource planning (ERP).
eComchain.history: eComchain is a cloud-based B2B and B2C e-commerce platform with integrated ERP capabilities, targeting mid-market and enterprise distributors and manufacturers; no notable ownership changes are documented.
eComchain.summary_abuse: eComchain is a B2B enterprise e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; its enterprise sales model and ERP integration context limits exposure to public fraud.

Ecommercen.description: Ecommercen is an ecommerce solution designed to facilitate online sales, manage transactions, and streamline the customer shopping experience.
Ecommercen.history: Ecommercen is a niche ecommerce SaaS platform for online store management. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Ecommercen.summary_abuse: Ecommercen is an ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signal.

EcomScout.description: EcomScout is a platform providing AI-powered tools for analytics, attribution, merchandising, and forecasting.
EcomScout.history: EcomScout is an AI-powered Shopify competitor intelligence and store analytics platform. It provides tools for analyzing competitor Shopify stores, tracking revenue estimates, discovering trending products, and analyzing app stacks. The browser extension is available for free on the Chrome Web Store.
EcomScout.summary_abuse: EcomScout is a Shopify analytics and competitor research tool. The platform collects publicly available store data. No notable abuse patterns are documented.

Ecomtrack.description: Ecomtrack is a software program that enables businesses to monitor inventory and sales.
Ecomtrack.history: Ecomtrack is an analytics and inventory tracking tool for ecommerce businesses. Its website shares the ecommercen.com domain, suggesting it may be a product of the same vendor. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Ecomtrack.summary_abuse: Ecomtrack is an ecommerce analytics SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

EcomX.description: EcomX is a platform that automatically generates SEO-optimized product, collection, and blog content using AI.
EcomX.history: EcomX is an AI-powered content generation SaaS for ecommerce, focused on automating SEO product and blog content. It emerged around 2022 as part of the AI marketing automation wave.
EcomX.summary_abuse: EcomX is an AI-powered ecommerce content tool with no documented abuse signal. Automated content generation tools can theoretically be used to produce spam content at scale, but no documented incidents are known.

Ecomz.description: Ecomz is a platform designed to create, manage, and grow online businesses through integrated ecommerce tools.
Ecomz.history: Ecomz was created in Lebanon in 2015 as a multilingual, multichannel ecommerce website builder. It operates in over 55 countries, serves over 7,000 merchants, and has helped generate over $50 million in sales. The platform supports 22+ languages and uses Odoo for ERP integration.
Ecomz.summary_abuse: Ecomz is a Lebanon-based ecommerce platform with no notable documented abuse. As a small ecommerce builder serving merchants primarily in the MENA region, standard ecommerce fraud risks apply but no specific incidents have been reported.

Econda.description: Econda is a provider of web analytics solutions for online shops, offering detailed insights into customer behaviour, website performance, and sales optimisation.
Econda.history: Econda is one of Germany's earliest web analytics providers for ecommerce, founded around 2000 and known for GDPR-compliant tracking that does not require user consent. With over 20 years of operation and more than 1,000 enterprise clients including KLiNGEL, Heine, and Continental, the company was later integrated into the DYMATRIX customer experience platform.
Econda.summary_abuse: Econda is a GDPR-compliant German analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform specializes in privacy-friendly web analytics for ecommerce and does not collect or broker personal data in ways associated with tracking abuse.

Ecovium.description: Ecovium is an end-to-end logistics company in Germany.
Ecovium.history: Ecovium was founded in 1999 in Neustadt am Rübenberge, Germany, and grew from 12 companies within the MHP Solution Group to be rebranded as Ecovium in 2021. In 2022, Mantis, a WMS provider, merged with Ecovium, creating a global logistics software group with over 400 employees at 21 locations serving about 4,000 customers worldwide.
Ecovium.summary_abuse: Ecovium is a B2B enterprise logistics software platform with no consumer-facing surface. No abuse signal is documented.

eCShop.description: eCShop is a Brazilian ecommerce platform designed for building and managing online shops.
eCShop.history: eCShop is a Brazilian ecommerce SaaS platform designed for building and managing online shops in the Brazilian market; no notable ownership changes or major events found in public records.
eCShop.summary_abuse: eCShop is a regional Brazilian ecommerce platform. Like any storefront builder it can host fake shops, but no specific abuse patterns or documented fraud incidents have been reported for this platform.

Ecwid.description: Ecwid is a shopping cart plugin that turns any existing website into an online store.
Ecwid.history: Ecwid was founded in 2009 by Ruslan Fazlyev in Ulyanovsk, Russia, as an ecommerce widget that could be embedded into any existing website. It grew to serve over 1 million merchants in 175 countries. In 2021, Ecwid was acquired by Lightspeed Commerce, a publicly traded Canadian POS and ecommerce company, for approximately $500 million USD.
Ecwid.summary_abuse: Ecwid is a mainstream ecommerce platform used by over a million merchants worldwide. As with any ecommerce platform, it can be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but Lightspeed maintains active trust and safety programs with documented AUP enforcement. The platform requires account registration and payment, limiting anonymous abuse.

EdgeCast.description: EdgeCast is a content delivery network (CDN) that accelerated and delivers static content to users around the world.
EdgeCast.history: EdgeCast Networks was founded in 2006 and acquired by Verizon Communications in December 2013, becoming part of Verizon Digital Media Services. After Verizon sold its media assets to Apollo Global Management, the CDN was renamed Edgecast in 2022. Limelight Networks subsequently acquired Edgecast and rebranded the combined company as Edgio in 2022. In February 2025, Edgecast technology was sold to Parler Cloud Technologies.
EdgeCast.summary_abuse: EdgeCast is a CDN infrastructure provider. Like all CDN services, it can be used to host and deliver malicious content, though major CDN providers maintain abuse teams and AUP enforcement. No specific documented abuse patterns distinguish EdgeCast from other enterprise CDN providers.

Edgemesh.description: Edgemesh is web acceleration platform for e-commerce brands
Edgemesh.history: Edgemesh is a US-based web performance company providing client-side website acceleration through a single JavaScript snippet. The technology uses service workers for intelligent client-side caching, prefetching, and adaptive security. Edgemesh has been featured as a Cloudflare and Fastly partner.
Edgemesh.summary_abuse: Edgemesh is a web performance acceleration tool embedded in websites via a JavaScript snippet. No notable abuse signal. The technology is used legitimately by ecommerce and content sites to improve load times.

EdgeOne.description: EdgeOne is an edge-service platform that delivers global content distribution, network acceleration, and integrated security protections for distributed applications.
EdgeOne.history: EdgeOne is Tencent Cloud's global edge security and CDN platform, launched around 2022, combining CDN acceleration, DDoS protection, WAF, and bot management. Backed by Tencent's 3,200+ global edge nodes and 400 Tbps bandwidth, it offers the unique feature of free unmetered CDN traffic for the China market.
EdgeOne.summary_abuse: EdgeOne is a Tencent CDN and security platform. As with all CDN services, it can deliver content for threat actors. However, Tencent is a large public company with abuse reporting mechanisms. Western intelligence agencies have raised concerns about Chinese cloud providers and data sovereignty, which is a consideration for enterprise deployments outside China.

Edgio.description: Edgio was a developer-focused edge platform offering delivery and compute features via Layer0, now discontinued and partially absorbed by Akamai.
Edgio.history: Edgio was originally Limelight Networks, founded in 2001 as a CDN provider. In 2022, Limelight acquired EdgeCast from Verizon Media and rebranded the combined company as Edgio. Edgio served over 935 enterprise customers including Amazon Prime Video, Sony, and Microsoft. In September 2024, Edgio filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Assets were sold piecemeal: Akamai acquired customer contracts for $125 million, and other assets including EdgeCast were acquired by separate buyers.
Edgio.summary_abuse: Edgio is a major CDN provider that filed for bankruptcy in 2024. As a CDN, it serves content for both legitimate and potentially malicious actors. Enterprise CDN providers maintain AUP enforcement teams, but CDNs in general can be used to deliver phishing kits, malware, and spam infrastructure. No specific abuse campaigns are uniquely attributed to Edgio.

eDirectory.description: eDirectory is an online directory software platform that enables the creation, management, and monetization of searchable, category-based listings for businesses, services, or professionals.
eDirectory.history: eDirectory is a US-based online directory software platform established in the early 2000s, enabling businesses to build and monetize directory listings; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
eDirectory.summary_abuse: eDirectory is a directory listing platform with no notable abuse signal; while directory sites can be used to post spam listings, there are no documented incidents of significant exploitation.

Edit-in-Place.description: Edit-in-Place is a tool that enables text editing directly on the page using AJAX-style updates without requiring a full page reload.
Edit-in-Place.history: Edit-in-Place is an early AJAX-style in-page text editing concept described in a 2005 article on 24ways.org. The pattern was popularized in early Web 2.0 applications and has since been superseded by more modern solutions. It requires jQuery.
Edit-in-Place.summary_abuse: Edit-in-Place is an early web editing technique with no meaningful abuse signal. As a frontend library pattern, it has no consumer-facing abuse surface.

Editor.js.description: Editor.js is a Javascript library which allows developers to implement a block base text editor with plugins on their page.
Editor.js.history: Editor.js was created by CodeX, a non-profit open-source development team, and first released publicly around 2017. It pioneered a block-based JSON content editing model, offering clean structured output rather than HTML markup. The project is maintained on GitHub and has accumulated over 27,000 stars, with a plugin ecosystem for blocks like images, lists, and code.
Editor.js.summary_abuse: Editor.js is an open-source block editor library with no meaningful abuse signal. As a frontend content editing library, it is embedded in applications and has no direct consumer-facing abuse surface.

EditPlus.description: EditPlus is a text, HTML, PHP, and Java editor with a Hex Viewer for Windows, offering Notepad replacement capabilities along with features for web authors and programmers.
EditPlus.history: EditPlus is a Windows text editor created by a Korean developer (ES-Computing) first released in the late 1990s. It has been a long-running shareware product popular among web developers and programmers for its syntax highlighting, FTP integration, and lightweight footprint compared to heavy IDEs.
EditPlus.summary_abuse: EditPlus is a desktop text editor with no online hosting component. No abuse signal is documented.

Edlio.description: Edlio is a platform that provides integrated K–12 teaching and communication solutions to support classroom management, student engagement, and school–home interaction.
Edlio.history: Edlio provides a K-12-focused CMS and communication platform for school districts, supporting over 19,000 schools. The company acquired SchoolInfo (backed by LLR Partners) to expand its capabilities. Edlio is a PowerSchool integration partner and is ADA/WCAG 2.1 compliant.
Edlio.summary_abuse: Edlio is a K-12 school website CMS with a controlled institutional customer base. No notable abuse signal. The platform serves school districts with locked-down access controls.

Edmingle.description: Edmingle is a course creation system that enables organizations to design, manage, and deliver online training programs.
Edmingle.history: Edmingle is an India-based SaaS LMS platform founded in Bengaluru, targeting coaching centers, instructors, and training businesses. The platform provides AI-powered analytics, multi-branch management, custom domains, and mobile app publishing for online academies.
Edmingle.summary_abuse: Edmingle is a legitimate education technology platform with no documented abuse pattern. The platform serves training businesses with controlled user access.

eDokan.description: eDokan is hosted ecommerce platform with drag-drop template builder and zero programming knowledge.
eDokan.history: eDokan is a hosted ecommerce platform with drag-and-drop template builder requiring no programming knowledge; it appears to be a small independent startup with no notable ownership changes documented.
eDokan.summary_abuse: eDokan is a hosted ecommerce store builder. Like any hosted storefront platform it could be used to create fake shops, but no specific documented abuse has been reported.

Edrone.description: Edrone is a purpose-built ecommerce CRM that includes marketing automation and voice commerce features.
Edrone.history: Edrone was founded in 2016 by Michal Blak and Rahim Blak in Krakow, Poland as a CRM and marketing automation platform for ecommerce. The company raised €4.6 million in funding led by INventures with participation from Mueller Medien, Atmos, and PortfoLion. The platform handles transactions worth over 3 billion euros monthly.
Edrone.summary_abuse: Edrone is an ecommerce CRM and marketing automation platform. As an email/SMS/WhatsApp marketing tool, it carries standard risks associated with mass messaging platforms, though the platform serves verified ecommerce businesses and requires proper authentication. No specific abuse incidents are documented.

Edugram.description: Edugram is an affiliate program and CPA network for monetizing educational traffic.
Edugram.history: Edugram is a CPA affiliate network and marketing automation platform focused on monetizing educational traffic. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Edugram.summary_abuse: Edugram operates in the affiliate marketing space targeting educational traffic, a sector with documented associations with misleading ads and lead-generation scams. Affiliate CPA networks in this vertical have historically been used to promote deceptive online education offers. No specific Edugram incidents are documented, but the category carries elevated abuse risk.

Edwiser Bridge.description: Edwiser Bridge is a Wordpress plugin facilitating the sale of Moodle courses with synchronized data and single sign-on capability.
Edwiser Bridge.history: Edwiser Bridge is a WordPress plugin developed by WisdmLabs (India) that bridges WordPress and Moodle, enabling course sales through WooCommerce with SSO and data synchronization between the two platforms. It operates as a freemium plugin with premium add-ons.
Edwiser Bridge.summary_abuse: Edwiser Bridge is a WordPress-Moodle integration plugin with no notable abuse signal. The plugin is used for legitimate LMS course sales and does not expose commonly abused features.

ef.js.history: ef.js is a minimal, lightweight JavaScript framework for building reactive web applications; it is an independent open-source project with a small community and limited activity in recent years.
ef.js.summary_abuse: ef.js is a small open-source JavaScript framework with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Effiliation.description: Effiliation is a performance marketing platform and affiliate network that facilitates partnerships between advertisers and publishers for commission-based promotion of products and services.
Effiliation.history: Effiliation is a French performance marketing and affiliate network founded in 1999 (later operating as Effinity). With over 25 years of operation, the platform connects advertisers with a network of 45,000+ active partners including publishers, influencers, and price comparison sites, and works with over 900 clients.
Effiliation.summary_abuse: Effiliation is a French affiliate network. As with all affiliate marketing platforms, there is inherent risk of fraudulent traffic, cookie stuffing, and incentivized clicks by low-quality affiliates. The platform has been operating for over 25 years with established advertiser relationships, suggesting above-average monitoring of partner quality.

Efilli.description: Efilli is a tool used to manage cookies on websites, providing users with data privacy control through GDPR compliance.
Efilli.history: Efilli is an ecommerce personalization platform providing behavioral analytics, product recommendations, and conversion optimization for online retailers. The platform targets ecommerce businesses with AI-powered personalization capabilities.
Efilli.summary_abuse: Efilli is an ecommerce personalization tool. As a behavioral tracking and personalization platform, it collects visitor data for targeting, which carries standard privacy risks. No specific abuse incidents are documented.

eGain Conversation.description: eGain Conversation is a customer engagement platform that centralises and manages customer interactions across multiple channels, including chat, email, social media, and messaging apps.
eGain Conversation.history: eGain was founded in 1997 and went public on Nasdaq. It is one of the pioneering enterprise customer engagement and live chat platforms. No major ownership changes.
eGain Conversation.summary_abuse: eGain Conversation is an enterprise customer engagement platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by large organizations for customer service operations and has no consumer-facing content-hosting features.

Eggflow.description: Eggflow is a platform that offers marketing automation and customer engagement tools to help increase ecommerce revenue.
Eggflow.history: Eggflow is a Shopify app developer offering a suite of marketing and sales optimization tools including upsell, review, traffic, giveaway, and popup apps. The company operates as Eggflow LLC and all apps are exclusively available on the Shopify App Store.
Eggflow.summary_abuse: Eggflow is a Shopify app suite with no notable abuse signal. The apps focus on marketing automation for Shopify stores and require merchant authentication. Some popup and traffic apps in this category can be used for aggressive marketing tactics, but no specific abuse incidents are documented for Eggflow.

Eggplant.description: Eggplant is a software testing and monitoring company.
Eggplant.history: Eggplant was founded in 2002 as TestPlant Ltd in the UK and developed the image-based automated testing tool eggPlant Functional. The company was acquired by Keysight Technologies in 2020 for approximately $330 million USD, and the product was rebranded as Keysight Eggplant Test. It uses AI-driven test generation with scriptless modeling.
Eggplant.summary_abuse: Eggplant is an enterprise software testing tool with no consumer-facing surface. No abuse signal is documented.

EKM.description: EKM is an all-in-one online store builder, with the company based in the UK.
EKM.history: EKM was founded in 2002 and has operated as the UK's leading domestic e-commerce platform for over 20 years, serving thousands of small and medium UK businesses. The platform provides fully hosted online stores with marketplace integrations for eBay, Amazon, and Etsy. In recent years the company evolved with a high-touch EKM Evolve plan including dedicated account management and ecommerce strategy support.
EKM.summary_abuse: EKM is a legitimate UK e-commerce platform with no known abuse incidents. The platform serves primarily small UK businesses and offers 14 days free access with UK-based support. As an e-commerce platform it has standard exposure to fraudulent storefronts but operates in a UK regulatory environment. PCI DSS compliant and GDPR-compliant.

eKomi.description: eKomi is a German supplier and product review service.
eKomi.history: eKomi was founded in 2008 by Michael Ambros and Gunther Schmidt in Berlin, Germany as a transaction-based customer review and ratings platform. The company grew through acquisitions including Reputami (travel reviews, 2016) and Dutch competitors Klantenvertellen.nl and Kiyoh, positioning itself as Europe's largest independent provider of verified transaction-based reviews.
eKomi.summary_abuse: eKomi is a B2B review collection platform with no notable abuse signal. Its widget embeds on merchant sites to display verified customer reviews. There is no consumer-facing open-access platform that fraudsters would exploit, and the verified review model limits the potential for fake review injection compared to open platforms.

eKonsilio.description: eKonsilio is a conversational management platform that enables organizations to design, deploy, and manage automated and human-assisted conversations across digital communication channels.
eKonsilio.history: eKonsilio is a French conversational management SaaS platform for automotive and other sectors; no notable ownership changes or major events are publicly documented.
eKonsilio.summary_abuse: eKonsilio is a B2B conversational management platform with no notable abuse signal; its enterprise-only positioning and live-chat focus do not expose it to significant fraud vectors.

Ektron CMS.description: Ektron CMS is developed on the Microsoft .NET framework and is 100% ASP.NET. In 2015 Ektron merged with EPiServer.
Ektron CMS.history: Ektron was founded in 1998 and was an enterprise CMS used by large organizations. In December 2014, both Ektron and EPiServer were acquired by Accel-KKR and merged in January 2015 under the EPiServer brand. EPiServer was subsequently sold to Insight Venture Partners for $1.16 billion in 2018, then rebranded as Optimizely in 2021 after acquiring the Optimizely A/B testing company. Ektron as a standalone product was deprecated, with customers migrated to Optimizely CMS.
Ektron CMS.summary_abuse: Ektron CMS is an enterprise content management system now deprecated under Optimizely. No abuse signal is documented for this platform.

Elastic APM.description: Elastic APM offers free and open application performance monitoring.
Elastic APM.history: Elastic APM is part of the Elastic Observability suite, built by Elastic NV (founded 2012, IPO 2018 on NYSE). The APM product was introduced around 2017 as the company expanded the Elastic Stack beyond search into full observability. Elastic controversially changed from the Apache 2.0 license to SSPL in January 2021, prompting AWS to fork Elasticsearch as OpenSearch.
Elastic APM.summary_abuse: Elastic APM is an application performance monitoring tool used by developers and operations teams. No notable abuse signal. As a backend monitoring tool, it has no consumer-facing surface that would make it a target for abuse.

Elastic Path.description: Elastic Path is a headless ecommerce system that integrates with enterprise ERP systems as middleware, enabling template-less commerce experiences.
Elastic Path.history: Elastic Path was founded in 2000 in Vancouver, Canada as a headless, API-first composable commerce platform. Originally focused on enterprise ecommerce, the company pivoted to a fully headless, microservices-based architecture for B2B enterprises. In 2025, Elastic Path expanded its platform with native search, CMS, and hosted frontend capabilities.
Elastic Path.summary_abuse: Elastic Path is an enterprise B2B headless commerce platform. No notable abuse signal. The platform serves large enterprise manufacturers and distributors with controlled access.

ElasticPress.description: ElasticPress is a hosting service that connects your WordPress site to search hosting.
ElasticPress.history: ElasticPress was created by the WordPress agency 10up and first released around 2014 as an open-source WordPress plugin that integrates with Elasticsearch for faster search. The plugin is maintained on GitHub and powers the ElasticPress.io SaaS service. It replaced default WordPress MySQL queries with Elasticsearch queries for dramatically better performance.
ElasticPress.summary_abuse: ElasticPress is a WordPress-Elasticsearch integration plugin with no notable abuse signal. As a backend search optimization tool, it has no consumer-facing abuse surface.

Elasticsearch.description: Elasticsearch is a search engine based on the Lucene library. It provides a distributed, multitenant-capable full-text search engine with an HTTP web interface and schema-free JSON documents.
Elasticsearch.history: Elasticsearch was created by Shay Banon, first released in 2010, and is the core engine of the Elastic Stack. Elastic NV was founded in 2012 and went public on NYSE in 2018 (ESTC). In January 2021, Elastic controversially changed the license from Apache 2.0 to SSPL and Elastic License 2.0, prompting AWS to fork the project as OpenSearch. The project has become a dominant distributed search and analytics engine.
Elasticsearch.summary_abuse: Elasticsearch is a foundational search and analytics engine with no direct consumer-facing abuse surface. As an infrastructure component, it is not typically abused in phishing or fraud schemes. However, misconfigured public-facing Elasticsearch instances have historically leaked sensitive data — a systemic ops risk rather than a platform abuse issue. Elastic provides security configuration guidance.

ElasticSuite.description: ElasticSuite is a merchandising suite for Magento and OroCommerce.
ElasticSuite.history: ElasticSuite was created by Smile, a French open-source integrator and the world's largest Magento agency. First released around 2014, it became the de facto industry-standard search extension for Magento 2/Adobe Commerce. The project has over 3 million downloads and offers both an open-source (GPL) and a premium (commercial) edition with advanced merchandising features.
ElasticSuite.summary_abuse: ElasticSuite is a Magento search and merchandising extension with no notable abuse signal. As a backend ecommerce module, it does not expose consumer-facing abuse vectors.

Elcodi.history: Elcodi was a Symfony-based PHP ecommerce component suite developed by a Spanish team and first released around 2014. It won the Best Open Source Project award at the Symfony Community Awards 2015. Despite this recognition, the project was later marked as End of Life (EOL) and abandoned, with the GitHub repository no longer maintained.
Elcodi.summary_abuse: Elcodi is an abandoned open-source ecommerce framework with no consumer-facing service. No abuse signal is documented.

Elcom.description: The Elcom Platform is a web content management and intranet portal software written in Microsoft ASP.NET and SQL Server by Elcom Technology.
Elcom.history: Elcom was founded in 1996 in Sydney, Australia by John Anstey within ATP Innovations, a university-backed tech incubator. It grew into an enterprise CMS and intranet platform used by Australian government and enterprise organizations. In 2011, the CMS product was rebranded to elcomCMS.
Elcom.summary_abuse: Elcom is an enterprise web CMS and intranet platform for Australian organizations with no notable abuse signal. It is a licensed B2B software product with no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Eleanor CMS.history: Eleanor CMS is a Russian PHP-based content management system. Limited public information is available about the project history.
Eleanor CMS.summary_abuse: Eleanor CMS is a niche Russian CMS. Self-hosted PHP CMS platforms can occasionally be exploited via vulnerabilities but have no documented specific abuse patterns for this product.

Element.description: Element is a Matrix-based end-to-end encrypted messenger and secure collaboration app.
Element.history: Element is a Matrix-based end-to-end encrypted messaging and collaboration client developed by Element (formerly New Vector), a UK company co-founded by the creators of the Matrix open-source protocol. New Vector/Element was spun out from Amdocs in 2017. The client became the reference implementation for Matrix and is used by governments and enterprises seeking sovereign, federated messaging.
Element.summary_abuse: Element is a privacy-focused E2E encrypted messenger. Due to the federated and self-hostable nature of Matrix, the network can theoretically host servers used by adversarial actors. However, Element.io itself is a legitimate communication product. Matrix federation does enable some abuse-resistant infrastructure that threat actors occasionally use.

Element UI.description: Element UI is an open-source component library specifically designed for Vue.js, offering a collection of pre-designed UI components such as buttons, forms, tables, and modals.
Element UI.history: Element UI is a component library for Vue.js created by Eleme.io (the food delivery company behind ele.me) and first released in 2016. It became one of the most popular Vue.js UI frameworks in China and globally. The original Element UI targeted Vue 2, while Element Plus was created for Vue 3. The GitHub repository has over 53,000 stars.
Element UI.summary_abuse: Element UI is an open-source Vue.js component library with no meaningful abuse signal. As a frontend library, it has no consumer-facing abuse surface.

Elementor.description: Elementor is a website builder platform for professionals on WordPress.
Elementor.history: Elementor was founded in 2016 by Yoni Luksenberg and Ariel Klikstein in Israel. It rapidly became the most popular WordPress page builder plugin, growing to over 10 million active installations. The company raised significant venture funding and expanded into hosting (Elementor Cloud), an AI assistant, and a full website builder product. In 2023, Elementor acquired Strattic for static WordPress hosting.
Elementor.summary_abuse: Elementor is the dominant WordPress page builder and is heavily used in phishing and fraud campaigns due to its ease of creating convincing fake pages. Security researchers regularly report Elementor plugin vulnerabilities that are exploited for site hijacking. The platform powers a significant share of fraudulent WordPress sites, though the vendor maintains a responsible disclosure program and publishes security advisories.

Elementor Addon Elements.description: Widgets and extensions to extend the Elementor Page Builder.
Elementor Addon Elements.history: Elementor Addon Elements is a WordPress plugin providing additional widgets and extensions for the Elementor page builder. It is one of many third-party Elementor addon plugins in the ecosystem, typically developed by smaller agencies.
Elementor Addon Elements.summary_abuse: Elementor Addon Elements is a WordPress plugin extending the Elementor page builder. As an Elementor addon, it shares the same WordPress security ecosystem risks. No specific documented abuse pattern.

Elementor Cloud.description: Elementor Cloud is a platform for creating and hosting WordPress websites with Elementor.
Elementor Cloud.history: Elementor Cloud is the managed WordPress hosting offering launched by Elementor Ltd. in 2021, bundling Elementor Pro with WordPress hosting. It allows users to build and host websites entirely within the Elementor ecosystem without a separate hosting provider.
Elementor Cloud.summary_abuse: Elementor Cloud is a managed WordPress hosting platform. As with all website hosting services, it can potentially be used to host fraudulent pages, though Elementor maintains abuse reporting procedures. The platform shares abuse risks with all mainstream WordPress hosting products.

Elementor Header & Footer Builder.description: Elementor Header & Footer Builder is a simple yet powerful WordPress plugin that allows you to create a layout with Elementor and set it as.
Elementor Header & Footer Builder.history: Elementor Header & Footer Builder is a free WordPress plugin developed by Brainstorm Force, the India-based team behind Astra Theme. Originally released around 2018, it allows users to design custom headers and footers using the Elementor page builder and apply them site-wide without any coding. It became widely popular, powering over 2 million websites. The plugin was eventually superseded by Ultimate Addons for Elementor from the same vendor, which extends the same functionality with additional widgets and performance improvements.
Elementor Header & Footer Builder.summary_abuse: A well-maintained WordPress plugin by Brainstorm Force, a reputable Indian vendor. No known abuse vectors, malware history, or privacy concerns. The plugin is open source under GPL and hosted on WordPress.org with a strong track record and millions of active installs.

ElementsKit.description: ElementsKit is an addon for Elementor that adds additional customisation options to the page builder.
ElementsKit.history: ElementsKit is an all-in-one Elementor addon developed by WPmet, a WordPress product company from Bangladesh. Launched around 2018, it provides 110+ Elementor widgets, a header-footer builder, mega menu builder, and a large template library. It gained a strong following among Elementor users, serving over 2 million websites worldwide. The plugin follows a freemium model with a paid Pro tier starting at $49/year and has been actively maintained for over seven years.
ElementsKit.summary_abuse: No known abuse or security incidents. ElementsKit is a legitimate WordPress plugin from WPmet with GPL licensing, hosted on WordPress.org. The company is transparent about products and pricing. Low risk for privacy abuse or malicious use.

Elenore.description: Elenore is an all-in-one business management software platform designed to streamline operations and support business growth.
Elenore.history: Elenore is an all-in-one business management CRM platform targeting small and growing businesses. Minimal corporate history is publicly available; the product appears to be a recently launched independent SaaS.
Elenore.summary_abuse: Elenore has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B CRM and business management tool with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous signup features that would attract fraud or phishing use.

Elevar.description: Elevar is an official Shopify Plus Partner data collection and marketing monitoring tool.
Elevar.history: Elevar was founded in 2018 and is based in the United States. The company built a server-side conversion tracking and tag management platform exclusively for Shopify merchants. Elevar helps direct-to-consumer brands maintain accurate conversion data in a privacy-first world by sending events directly to platforms like Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, and Klaviyo via their APIs. The company grew to serve over 6,500 DTC brands including Vuori and SKIMS, and has 4.6 stars on the Shopify App Store.
Elevar.summary_abuse: Elevar is a conversion tracking SaaS used by thousands of ecommerce brands. The platform collects significant amounts of customer behavioral and conversion data for advertising purposes, which creates privacy considerations. No known malicious activity or abuse patterns. The core use case is marketing analytics, which involves personal data flows to ad platforms.

Eleventy.description: Eleventy (11ty) a simpler static site generator.
Eleventy.history: Eleventy (11ty) is a zero-configuration static site generator created by Zach Leatherman in 2018, written in JavaScript. Leatherman was inspired by Jekyll but wanted a Node.js-native solution without requiring a JavaScript framework. The name came from a childhood mispronunciation of eleven. Eleventy distinguishes itself by supporting over 10 templating languages and producing zero client-side JavaScript by default. Version 3.0 was released in October 2024. In September 2024, Eleventy was acquired by Font Awesome to ensure its long-term sustainability.
Eleventy.summary_abuse: Eleventy is a static site generator with no client-side runtime. No known abuse patterns. It generates static HTML files which are entirely determined by the developer. No tracking, no data collection, and no server-side risk surface. Very low abuse potential.

Elevio.description: Elevio is a self-service support platform for SaaS that delivers contextual knowledge to customers, support agents, and internal teams.
Elevio.history: Elevio was founded in 2015 in Melbourne, Australia by Chris Duell and Matt Trimarchi. The company built a knowledge management and self-service support platform that integrated contextual help into web applications, bridging the gap between customer support and knowledge management. Elevio raised backing from Australian VCs Blackbird and AirTree Ventures. In February 2021, Elevio was acquired by Dixa, a Danish customer support platform, for approximately US$15 million.
Elevio.summary_abuse: No known abuse signals. Elevio is a legitimate knowledge management SaaS now owned by Dixa, used for customer support workflows. No history of malicious use, phishing infrastructure abuse, or data privacy incidents.

Elexio.description: Elexio is a software solution designed to manage church databases and websites.
Elexio.history: Elexio provides church management software (ChMS) serving churches in all 50 US states and 33 countries. The company originated from a merger of a church website company and one of the first church database companies. In 2015, Elexio joined Ministry Brands, a larger church technology platform. The product offers community ChMS, online giving, live streaming, accounting, and website management as integrated modules for churches of all sizes.
Elexio.summary_abuse: No known abuse patterns. Elexio serves the niche church management market, operating under Ministry Brands. The platform handles donor data and congregant information, which carries standard data protection obligations. No evidence of security incidents or misuse.

Elfsight.description: Elfsight is an all-in-one platform offering 70+ customisable widgets for websites.
Elfsight.history: Elfsight was founded in 2012 in Yerevan, Armenia as a bootstrapped SaaS company. It provides no-code, embeddable website widgets for 2.5 million website owners worldwide. The company grew entirely without external funding, reaching $3.9M annual revenue with a team of 35 employees. Its widgets span social media feeds, forms, reviews, chat buttons, and over 80 other types. The company rebranded in part to incorporate AI-driven widget capabilities.
Elfsight.summary_abuse: Elfsight widgets are third-party JavaScript embeds that load from Elfsight servers, which means they have access to visitor data on host websites. This is a common privacy concern for widget-based SaaS products. No known security incidents but the embed model creates data flow dependencies. Widgets can introduce tracker-equivalent behavior depending on configuration.

Elgg.description: Elgg is a social networking engine, delivering the building blocks for creating social networks and applications.
Elgg.history: Elgg is an open source social networking engine founded in 2004 by Ben Werdmuller and Dave Tosh, who combined their backgrounds in web communities and online education. Originally built as a social approach to e-learning, Elgg evolved into a general-purpose social networking platform used by organizations including Oxfam, Harvard, and Stanford. It is dual-licensed under GPL and MIT. The project has been maintained by a community of contributors since its founding.
Elgg.summary_abuse: Elgg is a self-hosted open source platform, so abuse potential depends on how individual deployments are managed. The platform itself is neutral code. Like any self-hosted social platform, specific deployments could host harmful content, but this is not an inherent characteristic of the software. Community development is transparent.

Elina PMS.description: Elina PMS is a software suite that includes a channel manager, booking engine, and property management system for handling reservations, availability, and operations.
Elina PMS.history: Elina PMS was established in 2008 as a property management system for hotels, serviced apartments, and short-term rentals. The platform integrates channel management, booking engine, guest communication, and reporting in a single cloud-based system. It has offices in Europe, the United States, and Australia, and connects to over 2,000 channels including Booking.com, Airbnb, and Expedia. The product targets independent hospitality operators and small hotel groups.
Elina PMS.summary_abuse: No known abuse history. Elina PMS serves a specialized hospitality market with standard SaaS data handling for guest and booking information. The platform operates in the regulated hospitality sector, with GDPR-relevant guest personal data flows. No security incidents or malicious use patterns documented.

EliseAI.description: EliseAI is an AI-powered conversational platform for business automation.
EliseAI.history: EliseAI is an AI-powered platform for property management automation, originally launched as Meet Elise as an AI leasing assistant for multifamily housing. The company uses natural language processing to automate prospect communication, tour scheduling, and resident workflows via voice, text, and email in multiple languages. EliseAI handles over 1.5 million customer interactions per year and has expanded into healthcare verticals. The company is venture-backed and headquartered in New York.
EliseAI.summary_abuse: EliseAI operates in property management, handling personal data of prospective renters and residents including contact info, housing inquiries, and scheduling. Privacy risk is moderate given the volume of personal communications processed by AI.
