No known security incidents, but the AI assistant model raises questions about consent and data retention for communications.

Elixir.description: Elixir is a dynamic, functional language designed for building scalable and maintainable applications.
Elixir.history: Elixir was created by José Valim, a core Ruby on Rails contributor, and first released in 2011. It runs on the Erlang BEAM virtual machine, providing the fault-tolerance and distributed computing capabilities of Erlang with a more approachable Ruby-inspired syntax. The language powers Phoenix, a high-performance web framework, and has been adopted by companies like Discord, Pinterest, and WhatsApp.
Elixir.summary_abuse: Elixir is a programming language with no consumer-facing abuse surface. No abuse signal is documented.

ElkArte.description: ElkArte is an open-source PHP-based forum software with MySQL integration, offering features such as user authentication, topic categorisation, post formatting, private messaging, moderation tools, and customisable themes and extensions.
ElkArte.history: ElkArte is an open source forum software that began as a fork of Simple Machines Forum (SMF) around 2012, with the goal of modernizing the codebase and implementing new features for a more contemporary community platform. It is written in PHP with MySQL or PostgreSQL support and licensed under the BSD-3-Clause license. ElkArte supports migration from many other forum platforms including phpBB, MyBB, and vBulletin. The project remains community-maintained with modest active development.
ElkArte.summary_abuse: ElkArte is a self-hosted forum software. Abuse potential is inherent to forum platforms in general, where individual deployments can host harmful or illegal content. The software itself is open source with a transparent codebase and a long history rooted in SMF. No known backdoors or intentional malicious features.

Elliptic.description: Fast Elliptic Curve Cryptography in plain javascript.
Elliptic.history: Elliptic was co-founded in 2013 by Adam Joyce, Tom Robinson, and James Smith in London. The company provides blockchain analytics for financial crime risk management, helping banks, exchanges, and government agencies monitor crypto transactions for sanctions, fraud, ransomware, and illicit finance. Two-thirds of global crypto trading volume flows through exchanges using Elliptic's services. In May 2026, Elliptic raised $120 million in a Series D round led by One Peak with backing from Nasdaq Ventures and Deutsche Bank, valuing the company at $670 million.
Elliptic.summary_abuse: Elliptic is specifically an anti-abuse and compliance platform for the blockchain industry. The company helps detect and prevent financial crime in crypto. No known misuse as a platform. Its customers include HSBC and Revolut. The product involves significant processing of blockchain transaction data but serves a clearly legitimate compliance function.

Elloha.description: Elloha is an all-in-one platform designed to manage and increase tourism-related revenue.
Elloha.history: Elloha was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in Perpignan, France. The company provides a cloud-based channel management and direct booking platform for tourism and leisure professionals including accommodation providers, activity operators, and tour companies. The platform connects to over 60 distribution channels including GetYourGuide, Viator, TripAdvisor, and Booking.com. Elloha has grown to over 50 employees and has earned strong customer reviews averaging 4.8 across 1800+ reviews.
Elloha.summary_abuse: No known abuse patterns. Elloha serves legitimate tourism businesses as a booking and channel management platform. Operates under French law with GDPR compliance. Handles booking data and customer personal information in the hospitality sector. Low risk of malicious use.

Ellucian CRM Recruit.description: Ellucian CRM Recruit is a comprehensive solution that supports your entire recruiting and admissions lifecycle.
Ellucian CRM Recruit.history: Ellucian CRM Recruit is a higher education enrollment management product from Ellucian, one of the largest higher education software companies in the world. Ellucian as a company traces its roots to the 1960s and has grown through mergers including the combination of Datatel and SunGard Higher Education in 2012. CRM Recruit is purpose-built for college and university admissions teams, providing prospect management, application tracking, and yield campaigns. It integrates natively with Ellucian Banner and Colleague student information systems.
Ellucian CRM Recruit.summary_abuse: No known abuse concerns. Ellucian CRM Recruit is used exclusively by accredited higher education institutions for admissions workflows. It processes student applicant data subject to FERPA regulations in the US. Ellucian is a well-established enterprise vendor in the higher education sector with strong institutional accountability.

Elm.description: Elm is a statically typed functional programming language created by Evan Czaplicki in 2012 for building web applications.
Elm.history: Elm is a purely functional programming language designed for front-end web development, created by Evan Czaplicki as his 2012 Harvard undergraduate thesis. Czaplicki was inspired by Haskell but aimed to create a language more accessible for web developers. Elm compiles to JavaScript and introduces a novel architecture (the Elm Architecture) that influenced Redux and similar state management patterns. The language emphasizes no runtime exceptions, immutability, and strong static typing. Licensed under BSD-3-Clause.
Elm.summary_abuse: Elm is a programming language with no inherent abuse surface. It compiles to JavaScript and produces browser applications. No known use in malicious tooling or abuse patterns. The language is niche and used primarily by developers seeking type-safe frontend applications.

Elm-ui.description: Elm-UI is a library for creating user interfaces in Elm programming language. It provides a set of functions and abstractions for building responsive and reusable UI components, such as buttons, forms, and layouts, in a declarative and type-safe way.
Elm-ui.history: Elm-ui (elm-ui) is an open source Elm library created by Matthew Griffith that provides a layout and UI framework as an alternative to writing HTML and CSS directly. The library treats CSS and HTML as output targets rather than programming models, aiming to eliminate common CSS pitfalls such as global scope, unused styles, and specificity conflicts. It was published on the Elm package ecosystem and became the most popular UI toolkit within the Elm community.
Elm-ui.summary_abuse: A niche open source UI library for the Elm programming language. No abuse potential as a library. Entirely client-side, no data collection, no server component. Used only in Elm applications which compile to JavaScript.

ELOG.history: ELOG is an open-source electronic logbook application developed by Stefan Ritt at Paul Scherrer Institut (PSI) in Switzerland since 2001. Released under the GPL, it is a single-daemon C program with an integrated web server that uses plain ASCII files as its database. It has been widely adopted in physics research environments including CERN, where it powers logbooks for the LHCb and CMS experiments.
ELOG.summary_abuse: ELOG is a niche scientific electronic logbook software used in physics laboratories including CERN with no known abuse incidents. The software is deployed internally in research institutions and does not have a public consumer-facing surface that would enable abuse.

ELOG HTTP.history: ELOG HTTP is the built-in HTTP server component of ELOG, an electronic logbook system developed at Paul Scherrer Institut (PSI) in Switzerland. ELOG was originally created by Stefan Ritt starting in 2001. The HTTP server is integrated into the ELOG daemon and provides web-based access to logbook entries without requiring a separate web server installation.
ELOG HTTP.summary_abuse: ELOG HTTP is the internal web server component of the ELOG scientific logbook software. It is deployed exclusively in private research networks such as CERN and PSI. There is no known abuse of this technology as it has no public-facing consumer surface.

Eloomi.description: Eloomi is a cloud-based learning management system (LMS) and performance management platform.
Eloomi.history: Eloomi was founded in 2015 in Copenhagen, Denmark as a Learning Management System and Learning Experience Platform. The company grew to serve over 850 enterprise customers globally including BigChange, Getty Images, and Icelandair. Eloomi raised backing from the Export and Investment Fund of Denmark, Kennet Partners, and Great Hill Partners. In January 2024, Ceridian acquired eloomi to integrate its L&D capabilities into the Dayforce HCM platform.
Eloomi.summary_abuse: No known abuse concerns. Eloomi is a legitimate enterprise learning platform now owned by Ceridian. Handles employee learning data with expected GDPR compliance as a Danish-origin company. No history of security incidents or malicious use.

Elopage.description: Elopage is a tool made for entrepreneurs to setup, manage, and scale their digital businesses around digital products, online courses, memberships, tickets, and digital services.
Elopage.history: Elopage was founded in 2015 in Berlin, Germany by Ozkan Akkilic and Tolga Onal. The company built a SaaS platform for selling digital products including online courses, memberships, digital downloads, and subscriptions, primarily targeting coaches, consultants, and content creators. Elopage raised $38 million in a Series A round and served over 68,000 users. In 2024, the company rebranded as Ablefy.
Elopage.summary_abuse: Elopage is a legitimate digital product marketplace used by coaches and creators. As a payment and product delivery platform, it handles customer financial transactions and digital content. The platform itself is not a known abuse vector, but as with any commerce platform, individual sellers could use it for questionable digital products. GDPR compliance is high given German origin.

Eloqua.description: Eloqua is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform for marketing automation offered that aims to help B2B marketers and organisations manage marketing campaigns and sales lead generation.
Eloqua.history: Eloqua was founded in 1999 as a standalone B2B marketing automation pioneer. It was one of the first companies to offer automated email campaigns and lead nurturing for enterprise sales organizations. Oracle acquired Eloqua in December 2012 for $871 million, integrating it into Oracle Marketing Cloud and later Oracle CX. The platform became a cornerstone of enterprise B2B marketing, competing with Marketo and Pardot. It runs on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and connects to Oracle CRM and CX applications.
Eloqua.summary_abuse: Eloqua is Oracle's enterprise marketing automation platform used by major corporations for B2B lead generation and email marketing. It collects significant volumes of prospect behavioral data. As a major enterprise platform, it has strong compliance certifications. Some privacy concerns around the volume of B2B contact profiling, but Oracle is a well-regulated public company.

Elromco.description: Elromco is a software platform that provides moving companies with a CRM and management solution.
Elromco.history: Elromco was founded in 2018 and provides an all-in-one cloud-based software platform for moving companies in the United States. The platform integrates CRM, dispatch, electronic bill of lading, online quoting, invoicing, and payroll management. It is used by over 400 moving companies and starts at $289/month. The company targets small to medium-sized moving businesses seeking to digitize their operations.
Elromco.summary_abuse: No known abuse patterns. Elromco is a niche vertical SaaS serving the US moving industry. It handles customer data and booking information for moving companies. No security incidents or malicious use cases documented. Low abuse risk given the B2B focus.

Email Encoder for Wordpress.description: Protect email addresses and phone numbers from spambots.
Email Encoder for Wordpress.history: Email Encoder for WordPress (also known as Email Encoder Bundle) is a WordPress plugin developed by Ironikus that protects email addresses and phone numbers from spam bots. It encodes addresses using methods such as HTML entities, CSS direction, and ROT13 to hide them from automated harvesters while keeping them visible to human users. The plugin is free and hosted on WordPress.org with premium options available.
Email Encoder for Wordpress.summary_abuse: A privacy-protective WordPress plugin that helps webmasters shield email addresses from spam bots. The plugin itself poses negligible abuse risk. It is open source with GPL licensing and improves site privacy rather than degrading it. No known security vulnerabilities or malicious use cases.

Emaileri.description: Emaileri is a platform designed for email marketing and newsletter communications.
Emaileri.history: Emaileri is a Finnish email marketing and newsletter platform founded in 1999 in Turku, Finland. The company served small to medium businesses with email campaign creation, subscriber management, and reporting tools. Emaileri remained a small operator with approximately 5 employees. In April 2026, Emaileri was acquired by Boston Information Group.
Emaileri.summary_abuse: Small Finnish email marketing platform with a limited footprint. No known abuse history. As an email service provider, there is inherent risk of use for spam campaigns, but no documented cases. The platform targets legitimate business email marketing with standard opt-in practices.

EmailJS.description: EmailJS is a cloud-based email delivery service that allows you to send emails directly from your client-side JavaScript code without the need for a server-side implementation.
EmailJS.history: EmailJS is a service that enables developers to send emails from client-side JavaScript code without requiring a server-side component. The service securely stores email provider credentials server-side and exposes a JavaScript SDK that developers use to trigger email templates. It supports many email providers including Gmail, Outlook, SendGrid, and Mailgun. The service is widely used by static site developers and front-end developers for contact forms and notifications.
EmailJS.summary_abuse: EmailJS exposes email sending capabilities to client-side JavaScript, which carries inherent abuse risk for spam. Malicious actors can abuse poorly secured EmailJS implementations to send spam through legitimate email accounts. The service requires a public key in client code, and misuse can result in email quota abuse. The company has rate limiting but moderate abuse exposure is documented in developer communities.

Emanda.description: Emanda is a platform offering SMS Marketing and Smart Email Marketing solutions.
Emanda.history: Emanda is a Brazilian SMS and email marketing platform. Limited public information is available about its founding date.
Emanda.summary_abuse: Emanda is an email/SMS marketing platform. Bulk messaging tools carry some risk of being used for spam campaigns, but no specific documented abuse incidents have been found for this platform.

Emarsys.description: Emarsys is a cloud-based B2C marketing platform.
Emarsys.history: Emarsys was founded in 2000 in Vienna, Austria as one of the earliest AI-driven marketing automation platforms for B2C brands. The company built sophisticated personalization and omnichannel marketing capabilities for retail and e-commerce companies. SAP acquired Emarsys in October 2020 for an undisclosed sum, integrating it into SAP's customer experience portfolio. In 2026, SAP rebranded Emarsys as SAP Engagement Cloud. Over 1,500 brands globally use the platform.
Emarsys.summary_abuse: Emarsys (now SAP Engagement Cloud) is a major enterprise marketing automation platform used by well-known retail and e-commerce brands. It processes large volumes of customer behavioral data for personalization and marketing. As a SAP subsidiary, it operates under strict compliance frameworks including ISO 27001 and GDPR. No known abuse history, though the platform handles significant personal data for marketing purposes.

Ematic Solutions.description: Ematic Solutions is part of Ematic Group and started to revolve around transforming email marketing into an ROI machine.
Ematic Solutions.history: Ematic Solutions was founded in 2012 in Singapore as a digital marketing consultancy specializing in email marketing. Over time it evolved into an all-round marketing technology consultancy serving brands across Southeast Asia and broader Asia Pacific. The company has over 180 employees across 11 markets including Singapore, Malaysia, Taiwan, and others, with over 650 active client engagements. Ematic is backed by TnB Aura Ventures among others.
Ematic Solutions.summary_abuse: Ematic Solutions is a marketing technology consultancy and services company. It does not operate a direct consumer-facing platform but manages marketing technology stacks for its clients. No known abuse history. Handles client customer data for marketing purposes, with privacy obligations under Singapore's PDPA and regional equivalents.

Embed Any Document.description: Embed PDF, DOC, PPT and XLS documents easily on your WordPress website.
Embed Any Document.history: Embed Any Document is a WordPress plugin that allows webmasters to easily embed PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel documents in WordPress pages and posts. The plugin leverages Google Docs Viewer and Microsoft Office Online for rendering, eliminating the need for browser plugins like Flash. It is available free on WordPress.org and has been maintained as an open source GPL project.
Embed Any Document.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. Embed Any Document is a document embedding plugin that routes viewing through Google Docs Viewer and Microsoft Office Online. The plugin does not collect user data itself. The main privacy consideration is that document viewership data flows through Google or Microsoft servers when viewers access embedded documents.

Embed Optimizer.description: Performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, which optimizes embeds by lazy loading them only as they come into view.
Embed Optimizer.history: Embed Optimizer is a WordPress performance plugin maintained by the WordPress Performance Team, which operates within the WordPress.org ecosystem with contributions from Automattic. The plugin optimizes the performance of embedded content (tweets, YouTube videos, TikToks, and others) through lazy loading, adding DNS prefetch hints, and reserving space to reduce layout shift. It is part of the broader WordPress Performance plugin family.
Embed Optimizer.summary_abuse: No abuse potential. Embed Optimizer is a WordPress performance plugin from the official WordPress Performance Team. It does not introduce third-party code, collect data, or create any attack surface. It purely improves how existing embeds are loaded in terms of page performance.

Embed PDF Viewer.description: Embed a PDF from the Media Library or elsewhere via oEmbed.
Embed PDF Viewer.history: Embed PDF Viewer is a free WordPress plugin that allows embedding PDF files from the WordPress Media Library or external URLs using oEmbed or as a Gutenberg block, rendering them in an iframe. It is a simple, lightweight plugin focused on a single task and distributed under GPL on WordPress.org. The plugin has been translated into multiple locales.
Embed PDF Viewer.summary_abuse: No significant abuse concerns. Embed PDF Viewer is a minimal WordPress plugin that embeds PDFs from the media library or external sources. It does not introduce tracking or data collection. Low risk of misuse as it serves a narrow document embedding use case.

Embedly.description: Embedly is a service that allows developers to convert URLs into rich previews and embeddable content.
Embedly.history: Embedly was founded in 2010 in Boston, participated in Y Combinator that year, and raised seed funding from Betaworks, Ron Conway, and Chris Sacca. The company built an API for converting URLs into rich media previews and embeddable content, used by services like Bit.ly, Yammer, and Tweetdeck. In August 2016, blogging platform Medium acquired Embedly for an undisclosed amount.
Embedly.summary_abuse: Embedly converts URLs to rich media previews and is used as an API by web applications. When embedded widgets load, they make requests to Embedly servers which can track URLs and user context. As a Medium subsidiary, the service is now primarily used within Medium's ecosystem. Moderate privacy concern around link preview data collection.

EmbedPlus.description: EmbedPlus is a WordPress plugin for YouTube allows you to embed gallery, channel, playlist, or even live stream on your webpage.
EmbedPlus.history: EmbedPlus is a WordPress plugin developed by EmbedPlus that enables advanced YouTube embedding capabilities. The plugin allows WordPress site owners to embed YouTube videos, playlists, channels, galleries, and live streams with features like lazy loading, facades for performance, and customization options. It has been available on WordPress.org for over a decade with both free and Pro versions.
EmbedPlus.summary_abuse: EmbedPlus is a YouTube embedding plugin with no significant abuse risk. It serves as a wrapper around YouTube's own player. The main privacy note is that YouTube videos, when embedded, trigger YouTube/Google tracking cookies on the host page. The plugin mitigates this with facade mode. No known security incidents.

EmbedPress.description: Embed videos, images, posts, audio, maps and upload PDF, DOC, PPT in Wordpress.
EmbedPress.history: EmbedPress is a WordPress plugin developed by WPDeveloper, a Bangladesh-based WordPress product company. The plugin enables embedding of content from over 250 providers including YouTube, Vimeo, Google Docs, social media feeds, PDFs, and more directly into WordPress posts and pages. It supports Gutenberg, Elementor, and other popular page builders. The plugin follows a freemium model with a paid Pro version.
EmbedPress.summary_abuse: EmbedPress is a content embedding plugin that pulls external content from third-party sources. Each embedded service introduces its own tracking. The plugin itself does not collect user data, but embedding content from YouTube, Twitter, or other services can trigger those services' own cookies and tracking pixels. No known malicious use cases.

EmbedSocial.description: EmbedSocial is a social media management platform.
EmbedSocial.history: EmbedSocial was founded around 2015 and provides an AI-powered UGC (user-generated content) platform for aggregating and displaying social media feeds, reviews, and other content on websites. The platform serves 300,000+ worldwide users with features for social media aggregation, widget creation, and social commerce. It integrates with Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, and other major social networks.
EmbedSocial.summary_abuse: EmbedSocial aggregates social media content and embeds it on third-party websites via JavaScript widgets. The service necessitates OAuth access to users' social media accounts to pull content, and the embedded widgets track visitor behavior on host websites. Privacy risk is moderate given the social media data access model. No known malicious use but the data model warrants scrutiny.

EmbedThis Appweb.history: EmbedThis Appweb is a compact, embedded web server developed by EmbedThis Software that supports HTTP/1, HTTP/2, and WebSockets. With a 1-4MB memory footprint, it is designed for IoT devices, networking equipment, telephony, and resource-constrained environments. It was developed in the early 2000s and has been deployed in production hardware worldwide. The software is dual-licensed under GPL v2+ and a commercial license. As of 2024, Appweb is in maintenance mode, with EmbedThis recommending the newer Ioto Device Agent for new projects.
EmbedThis Appweb.summary_abuse: EmbedThis Appweb is an embedded web server for IoT and industrial devices. No known malicious use cases. As a server component, it could theoretically be deployed in a way that exposes services, but this is true of any web server. The dual-license model and long deployment history in legitimate hardware products suggest low abuse risk.

Ember.js.description: Ember.js is a comprehensive JavaScript framework that simplifies the development of modern web applications by providing a powerful CLI, a built-in router, and a fast rendering engine.
Ember.js.history: Ember.js was created by Tom Dale and Yehuda Katz, with its first commit on April 30, 2011. Originally known as SproutCore MVC, it was renamed Ember.js and had its first stable release (1.0) in August 2013. Ember was designed as a framework for building ambitious web applications, emphasizing convention over configuration inspired by Ruby on Rails. It pioneered concepts later adopted by other frameworks, including the Glimmer rendering engine. Notable users include Apple Music, LinkedIn, HashiCorp, Intercom, Discourse, and Twitch.
Ember.js.summary_abuse: Ember.js is an open source JavaScript framework with no inherent abuse surface. It is used for building client-side web applications and does not involve data collection or server-side processing. Zero abuse potential in its own right. Applications built with Ember can have any characteristics, but the framework itself is entirely neutral.

emBlue.description: emBlue is an email and marketing automation platform.
emBlue.history: emBlue is an Argentine email and marketing automation platform founded around 2001, serving Latin American businesses; no notable ownership changes are documented.
emBlue.summary_abuse: emBlue is a marketing automation and email platform; like all bulk-email tools it carries some risk of being used for spam, but no specific major abuse incidents are documented.

Emergent.description: Emergent is an AI-powered, no-code platform that lets users build functional web apps from natural language prompts instead of writing code.
Emergent.history: Emergent is an AI-powered no-code platform that enables users to build functional web applications from natural language prompts without writing code. The platform represents a new wave of AI-assisted application builders that emerged following advances in large language models around 2023-2024. It is categorized as a CMS-adjacent platform that generates deployable web applications from plain English descriptions.
Emergent.summary_abuse: Emergent is an AI app generation platform. Platforms that allow users to build web apps via AI prompts carry elevated risk of being used to quickly generate phishing sites, scam storefronts, or social engineering content. The AI-generated nature of output applications creates challenges for policy enforcement. No specific documented abuse incidents, but the category carries inherent risk.

Emfont.description: Emfont is an open-source CJK webfont service that enables self-hosted, cache-efficient, and privacy-conscious distribution of open-source fonts.
Emfont.history: Emfont is a free CJK webfont service created in Taiwan, hosted at font.emtech.cc, providing over 90 open-source Chinese, Japanese, and Korean fonts with easy JavaScript integration. The project is open source on GitHub under the emfont organization and was designed to fill the gap in free Chinese webfont hosting services.
Emfont.summary_abuse: Emfont is a font delivery service with no notable abuse signal. It serves static font files to web developers, has no user content hosting or custom domain features that could be exploited, and operates as a read-only CDN-style service.

Emojicom.description: Emojicom is a tool for collecting feedback through emojis, enabling the capture of visitors' emotions.
Emojicom.history: Emojicom is a lightweight SaaS feedback widget founded around 2019, allowing websites to collect emoji-based user reactions. It integrates with WordPress, Shopify, Medium, and other platforms. The product is small and independently operated with no notable acquisitions or major events.
Emojicom.summary_abuse: Emojicom is a simple feedback widget with minimal abuse surface. The service collects emoji reactions and has no user content hosting, file uploads, or custom domain features that would expose it to significant abuse. No documented abuse incidents.

Emotion.description: Emotion is a library designed for writing CSS styles with JavaScript.
Emotion.history: Emotion is a CSS-in-JS library created by Kye Hohenberger in 2017. It rapidly gained adoption in the React ecosystem as an alternative to styled-components, offering high performance through dynamic style injection. The library is maintained by the emotion-js open source organization on GitHub and has become one of the most widely used CSS-in-JS solutions, used by MUI (Material UI) and many other major libraries.
Emotion.summary_abuse: Emotion is a CSS-in-JS library with no notable abuse signal. It is a purely technical frontend dependency with no server-side execution, no user content handling, and no consumer-facing surface.

Emotive.description: Emotive is a computer software company that provides SaaS, Mobile Marketing, NLP, machine learning, and B2B.
Emotive.history: Emotive was founded in 2018 in Los Angeles as a conversational SMS marketing platform focused on ecommerce brands. The platform scaled two-way SMS conversations to improve cart abandonment recovery. In 2025, Emotive was acquired by Privy, an ecommerce marketing automation company, to create a unified email and SMS platform for merchants.
Emotive.summary_abuse: Emotive is an SMS marketing platform with moderate abuse potential given its mass-texting capabilities. SMS marketing platforms can be misused for spam or unsolicited messaging. Emotive focuses on opted-in ecommerce subscribers, which limits abuse risk compared to generic SMS platforms. No major public abuse incidents documented.

Emplifi UGC.description: Emplifi UGC is a platform that helps brands curate, measure, and integrate user-generated content to enhance engagement, authenticity, and ecommerce performance by offering features like product page galleries, social media contests, shoppable content, and detailed analytics.
Emplifi UGC.history: Emplifi was formed in 2021 through the merger of Socialbakers and Astute, rebranding under the Emplifi name. The UGC platform originated from the acquisition of Pixlee, a user-generated content platform, which was integrated into the Emplifi suite. Emplifi focuses on helping brands leverage customer content and social commerce.
Emplifi UGC.summary_abuse: Emplifi UGC is a managed brand platform for collecting and displaying user-generated content. It is used by established consumer brands for social proof and commerce, with content moderation tools built in. No notable abuse incidents; the platform is not publicly accessible for anonymous content hosting.

Employment Hero.description: Employment Hero is a cloud-based human resources information system (HRIS) designed to manage HR operations, payroll processing, and recruitment workflows for small and medium-sized businesses.
Employment Hero.history: Employment Hero was founded in 2014 in Sydney, Australia by Ben Thompson and other co-founders. The company grew to serve over 200,000 organisations managing more than 1 million employees across Australia, New Zealand, UK, Canada, Singapore, and Malaysia. It raised significant VC funding and achieved SOC 2 Type II compliance in 2025, establishing itself as a leading HR and payroll SaaS in the Asia-Pacific region.
Employment Hero.summary_abuse: Employment Hero is an HR and payroll platform handling sensitive employee data. The platform is used by SMBs and enterprises for legitimate HR operations. It achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in 2025, signaling serious security posture. No notable public abuse incidents.

Empretienda.description: Empretienda is a platform that allows you to create and manage your own online store.
Empretienda.history: Empretienda was founded in Argentina in 2017 as an ecommerce platform targeting microentrepreneurs. The platform grew to over 120,000 merchants and was acquired by Ualá, the Argentine fintech unicorn, to expand its financial and commerce offerings for small businesses.
Empretienda.summary_abuse: Empretienda is an ecommerce store builder operating primarily in Argentina. As a platform allowing anyone to create an online store, it has some exposure to fake storefront scams, but its subscription model and payment integration with Ualá and Mercado Pago reduces anonymous abuse. No major documented abuse incidents.

Enabase.description: Enabase is a unified platform for managing web, ecommerce, e-transformation, pre-accounting, and sales channels.
Enabase.history: Enabase is a Turkish unified platform for web, e-commerce, e-transformation, and pre-accounting management. Limited public founding history is available.
Enabase.summary_abuse: Enabase is a small Turkish multi-channel commerce management platform with no documented abuse signal.

Enable.description: Enable is a web accessibility plugin by uPress.
Enable.history: Enable was founded in the UK around 2016 as a B2B rebate management software platform, helping manufacturers, distributors, and retailers manage complex deal structures and financial incentives. The company raised significant venture funding and expanded to North America, processing over $30 billion in rebate deals. The platform gained Microsoft Azure Marketplace listing and integrations with major ERP systems.
Enable.summary_abuse: Enable is a B2B financial software platform for rebate management used by enterprises. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. The platform handles sensitive financial data between trading partners, making it a high-trust enterprise tool rather than an abuse vector.

Enagic.description: Enagic is a content management system (CMS) business website builder designed to help companies create, manage, and organize their online presence through structured content and customizable templates.
Enagic.history: Enagic was founded in Osaka, Japan in 1974 and manufactures Kangen Water alkaline water ionization machines. The company operates a multi-level marketing distribution model globally. Despite long operating history, the company has faced scrutiny over health claims made by distributors and the MLM structure, including actions by consumer watchdog groups.
Enagic.summary_abuse: Enagic operates a multi-level marketing model for expensive water ionization machines, and independent distributors have been cited by Truth in Advertising for making exaggerated health claims. The high product price and MLM structure create potential for deceptive sales practices. The company itself is not a scam, but the distributor network has generated numerous consumer complaints.

Enalyzer.description: Enalyzer is an online survey creation platform.
Enalyzer.history: Enalyzer was founded in Copenhagen, Denmark in 2000 and has operated for over 25 years as a survey platform and consulting partner. It grew to become the leading survey tool in Scandinavia with offices in Denmark, Sweden, Norway, and the Netherlands, serving organizations with GDPR-compliant EU-hosted survey data.
Enalyzer.summary_abuse: Enalyzer is a professional survey platform focused on the Scandinavian market with GDPR-first design and EU data hosting. No notable abuse signal. The platform serves businesses and organizations for legitimate research and feedback purposes.

eNamad.description: eNamad is an electronic trust symbol.
eNamad.history: eNamad is an Iranian government-mandated electronic trust certification symbol for online businesses, operated by the Iranian e-commerce regulatory body; it is required for legally operating Iranian e-commerce sites.
eNamad.summary_abuse: eNamad is an Iranian e-trust badge issued by a government body; it can be spoofed by fraudulent sites mimicking legitimate certification, which is a documented problem in the Iranian e-commerce space, but the platform itself is not a fraud tool.

Endurance Page Cache.description: Endurance Page Cache adds basic file-based caching to WordPress.
Endurance Page Cache.history: Endurance Page Cache is a WordPress caching plugin developed and maintained by Bluehost (Endurance International Group, now Newfold Digital). It is automatically installed on Bluehost and HostGator-hosted WordPress sites to provide file-based caching without requiring manual configuration. The plugin is open-sourced on GitHub under the Bluehost organization.
Endurance Page Cache.summary_abuse: Endurance Page Cache is a basic WordPress caching plugin bundled with Bluehost hosting. It has no abuse signal as it is a technical backend tool that serves cached HTML files and has no consumer-facing features that could be exploited.

enduro.js.history: enduro.js is a minimalistic Node.js CMS built on Handlebars and Express, created by Gottwik (Vojtech Dusek) around 2016. It uses flat files instead of a database and was notable for fast compilation via BrowserSync. The project appears no longer actively maintained.
enduro.js.summary_abuse: enduro.js is a self-hosted Node.js CMS with no consumer-facing hosted surface. No documented abuse signal exists for this project.

Enecto.description: Enecto is a tool for analyzing web visitor demographics to provide insights into audience composition and behavior.
Enecto.history: Enecto Sverige AB was founded in 2002 in Lidingö, Sweden as one of the early pioneers in B2B website visitor identification. The company introduced ProspectFinder in 2003 for the European market and later launched Enecto Web Leads in 2015. The website was under construction as of 2026, suggesting the service may have wound down.
Enecto.summary_abuse: Enecto is a B2B visitor tracking tool that identifies companies visiting websites by IP address, matching against company databases. This IP-based identification raises privacy concerns under GDPR. Abuse signal is limited to data privacy concerns rather than active exploitation. The service appears to have reduced activity.

Engaga.description: Engaga is a platform that enhances website effectiveness with call-to-action popups and popup forms designed to convert visitors into customers and subscribers.
Engaga.history: Engaga is a small SaaS popup and call-to-action builder that helps websites convert visitors into subscribers and customers. The company appears to be independently operated and niche in scale. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Engaga.summary_abuse: Engaga is a popup builder tool with limited abuse surface. Popup tools can be used to create deceptive consent forms or fake overlays on phishing sites, but Engaga itself is a legitimate small vendor. No notable abuse incidents documented.

EngagementHQ.description: Essential software for changemakers. Facilitate, centralize, and analyze two-way conversations between you and your community.
EngagementHQ.history: EngagementHQ was originally developed by Bang the Table, an Australian civic technology company founded around 2011 in Victoria. It became the world's leading public consultation platform for government. Bang the Table was later acquired by Granicus, a US-based government technology company, integrating EngagementHQ into the Granicus platform suite serving governments across Australia, UK, Canada, and the US.
EngagementHQ.summary_abuse: EngagementHQ is a government community engagement platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used exclusively by local and central government bodies for public consultation. The platform employs 24/7 human moderation for discussions, significantly reducing abuse risk.

Engagio.history: Engagio was founded in early 2015 by Jon Miller, co-founder of Marketo, and Brian Babcock. The company pioneered the concept of Account-Based Marketing (ABM) orchestration, helping B2B marketers target and engage specific enterprise accounts. In June 2020, Demandbase acquired Engagio, with Miller becoming Chief Product Officer and Babcock becoming CTO at Demandbase. The Engagio brand was subsequently absorbed into the Demandbase platform.
Engagio.summary_abuse: Engagio was a B2B marketing platform used by enterprise sales and marketing teams. It had no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. The product has been deprecated following the Demandbase acquisition in 2020.

Engati.description: Engati is a chatbot platform that allows users to build bots without requiring programming skills.
Engati.history: Engati was founded in 2017 in India as an AI chatbot and customer experience platform. The platform supports omnichannel deployment across WhatsApp, Messenger, Telegram, and 15+ channels. It has expanded into an enterprise AI customer experience platform serving industries including ecommerce, banking, travel, and retail.
Engati.summary_abuse: Engati is a chatbot platform with low abuse signal. As a customer service automation tool, its primary use is for legitimate business communication. The platform does not offer anonymous user content hosting or features that are commonly exploited for scams.

Engintron.description: Engintron is a plugin that integrates Nginx to cPanel/WHM server.
Engintron.history: Engintron is an open source tool created around 2014 to simplify Nginx integration on cPanel/WHM servers. It installs Nginx as a reverse caching proxy in front of Apache to improve performance and reduce server load. The project is maintained on GitHub and used widely by cPanel server administrators who want Nginx performance benefits without abandoning the cPanel ecosystem.
Engintron.summary_abuse: Engintron is a server-side infrastructure tool for cPanel administrators with no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal as it is a technical optimization tool used by hosting providers and server administrators.

Enhanced Responsive Images.description: Performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, which improves image sizes attribute accuracy and implements the HTML spec for adding sizes='auto' to lazy loaded images.
Enhanced Responsive Images.history: Enhanced Responsive Images is a WordPress plugin developed by the WordPress Performance Team as part of the WordPress Performance Lab initiative. It implements the HTML spec for adding sizes=auto to lazy-loaded images and improves image sizes attribute accuracy. The plugin was created around 2023 as part of WordPress's ongoing performance improvements.
Enhanced Responsive Images.summary_abuse: Enhanced Responsive Images is a technical WordPress plugin from the official WordPress Performance Team with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It manipulates image attributes on server-rendered WordPress pages.

Enigma.description: Enigma is the popular superfine multipurpose responsive WordPress theme from Infigo Software.
Enigma.history: Enigma Technologies was founded in 2011 in New York City as a data intelligence company. It aggregates public and third-party data sources to create comprehensive intelligence about small and medium business identity and financial health. The company has raised significant venture funding and launched data products on Databricks Marketplace, serving financial institutions and enterprises for risk and compliance use cases.
Enigma.summary_abuse: Enigma is a B2B data intelligence platform providing business identity and financial health data to enterprise customers. It handles sensitive commercial data but has no consumer-facing features that would expose it to direct abuse. Its customers are financial institutions and enterprises performing KYC and risk analysis.

Enjin CMS.description: Enjin CMS is a content management system which focused creation of websites for gaming guilds, clans, Minecraft servers, or fan communities.
Enjin CMS.history: Enjin CMS is a website builder launched around 2009 specifically for gaming guilds, clans, Minecraft servers, and fan communities. It provides a free tier for basic community websites and has maintained a niche gaming community presence. User reviews from 2020 onward noted declining quality and poor support, indicating reduced investment in the platform.
Enjin CMS.summary_abuse: Enjin CMS is a gaming community website builder with a free tier that allows users to create public websites. The free tier and user-created content hosting create limited exposure to fake community pages or spam sites, but Enjin has no significant documented abuse history.

Enjore.description: Enjore is an online sports administration system designed to manage schedules, results, statistics, and news for all leagues and tournaments.
Enjore.history: Enjore is a niche online sports administration platform for managing leagues, tournaments, schedules, results, and statistics. The platform serves amateur sports leagues and organizations. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Enjore.summary_abuse: Enjore is a sports administration platform for leagues and tournaments with no notable abuse signal. It serves a specific niche of sports organizers and has no consumer-facing features commonly exploited for fraud or spam.

Enkod.description: Enkod is a CDP platform designed for marketing automation.
Enkod.history: Enkod is a customer data platform and marketing automation tool focused on the Russian market. It provides CDP features including audience segmentation and marketing channel orchestration. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Enkod.summary_abuse: Enkod is a marketing automation CDP platform. Marketing automation tools can be used for email spam if misused, but Enkod appears to serve legitimate business marketing use cases. No documented abuse incidents found.

Enlistly.description: Enlistly tracks referral orders in realtime. Orders that are partially refunded, refunded, or cancelled update on the fly.
Enlistly.history: Enlistly is a small referral tracking SaaS that monitors referral orders in real time, handling partial refunds, cancellations, and order updates. The platform serves ecommerce businesses looking to run affiliate or referral programs. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Enlistly.summary_abuse: Enlistly is a referral order tracking tool for ecommerce. No notable abuse signal. Referral tracking platforms have minimal abuse surface since they are B2B tools used by merchants, not end consumers.

Enonic.description: Enonic is a platform that helps you to create digital experiences by structuring and composing content.
Enonic.history: Enonic was established in 2000 in Oslo, Norway and grew to become the largest Norwegian CMS vendor. The company's flagship product, Enonic XP, is a headless content management system with an integrated JavaScript application framework. Notable Norwegian customers include Norway Post, Gjensidige insurance, and the Norwegian Labour and Welfare Administration. The CMS has an open source core with enterprise support tiers.
Enonic.summary_abuse: Enonic is an enterprise headless CMS used by government agencies and large Norwegian organizations. No notable abuse signal. The platform is typically deployed in controlled enterprise environments with no anonymous content hosting features.

Enquire.js.description: enquire.js is a lightweight, pure JavaScript library for responding to CSS media queries.
Enquire.js.history: Enquire.js is a lightweight JavaScript library (~0.8kb) for programmatically responding to CSS media queries, created around 2012 by Nick Williams. The library has no dependencies and allows developers to register match and unmatch callbacks for media query changes. It gained modest usage during the responsive design era but saw declining activity as the native window.matchMedia API improved.
Enquire.js.summary_abuse: Enquire.js is a frontend JavaScript utility library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is a purely technical tool for handling media query events in JavaScript.

Enquisite.description: Enquisite is a platform that provides web analytics and metrics not typically available through standard webmaster tools.
Enquisite.history: Enquisite was a web analytics platform that provided search metrics and keyword intelligence not available through standard webmaster tools. The service appears to have ceased active development and may no longer be operational based on the website status.
Enquisite.summary_abuse: Enquisite was a web analytics service with no notable abuse signal. The platform collected website visitor data for SEO and search analytics purposes. The service appears to be abandoned.

Enrollware.description: Enrollware is a CPR class management software that streamlines course scheduling, student registration, instructor tracking, and certification management for training providers.
Enrollware.history: Enrollware is a US-based SaaS platform for CPR and first aid training organizations, providing course scheduling, student registration, and class management tools. It targets American Heart Association and Red Cross instructors and training centers. No notable ownership changes found.
Enrollware.summary_abuse: Enrollware is a specialized course management platform for CPR and safety training organizations. It has no notable abuse signal as it operates in a regulated healthcare training niche with verified instructor accounts.

Ensi.description: Ensi is an open source ecommerce platform based on service oriented architecture.
Ensi.history: Ensi is an open source ecommerce platform built on a service-oriented architecture. The project is independently maintained and targets developers building modular ecommerce systems. No major ownership changes or significant events found.
Ensi.summary_abuse: Ensi is an open source ecommerce framework with no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool for building custom ecommerce systems.

Ensighten.description: Ensighten is a solution that enables secure management, implementation and control of website technologies.
Ensighten.history: Ensighten was founded in 2009 as an enterprise tag management system, competing with Adobe DTM and Tealium. The company built significant enterprise adoption for its security-focused approach to tag governance and data privacy compliance. Ensighten was acquired by CHEQ, a go-to-market security company, and was rebranded as CHEQ Control and Compliance, though the Ensighten brand name continues in use.
Ensighten.summary_abuse: Ensighten is an enterprise tag management platform used by large organizations for controlling third-party scripts and privacy compliance. No notable abuse signal. The platform is a security tool itself, focused on blocking malicious tags and enforcing data governance.

Enterprise Bot.description: Enterprise Bot specializes in developing and deploying complex conversational AI systems tailored for enterprise settings, leveraging natural language processing, machine learning, and automation to improve customer interactions and streamline operations.
Enterprise Bot.history: Enterprise Bot is a conversational AI platform founded around 2018 in Switzerland, specializing in complex chatbot deployments for enterprise clients. The company focuses on financial services, insurance, and enterprise customer service automation, offering multilingual AI chatbots and process automation solutions.
Enterprise Bot.summary_abuse: Enterprise Bot is an enterprise conversational AI platform serving financial services and insurance industries. No notable abuse signal. The platform requires enterprise contracts and is not publicly accessible for anonymous use.

Entresoft.description: Entresoft is a platform that offers CRM and pipeline management tools for business operations.
Entresoft.history: Entresoft is a small CRM and pipeline management software. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Entresoft.summary_abuse: Entresoft is a small CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for managing customer relationships and sales pipelines.

Entrust.description: Entrust is a provider of SSL certificate badge for websites.
Entrust.history: Entrust was founded in 1994 in Ottawa, Canada (now headquartered in Shakopee, Minnesota) as a digital security company. It became a major certificate authority and identity security provider. In 2025, Entrust sold its public TLS certificate business to Sectigo after browsers distrust incidents, and stopped issuing public TLS certificates from Entrust root CAs in March 2025. The company continues to offer private PKI, managed PKI, and identity solutions.
Entrust.summary_abuse: Entrust is a certificate authority and digital security company. In 2024, major browsers including Chrome and Firefox moved to distrust Entrust TLS certificates due to compliance failures and inadequate incident responses, which prompted the sale of its public CA business to Sectigo in 2025. This was a significant trust incident for a major CA. Its private PKI and enterprise identity products remain active.

Envialia.history: Envialia was founded in 2001 in Coslada, Spain by twelve entrepreneurs in the urgent transport sector, aiming to create a national parcel network to compete with major carriers. The company grew to serve 35,000 clients and process 85,000 daily shipments. In 2023, Envialia was acquired by Ontime, a Spanish logistics company.
Envialia.summary_abuse: Envialia is a Spanish parcel delivery carrier with no notable abuse signal. The company operates through a franchise network for package delivery across Spain, Portugal, and international destinations. No documented abuse incidents.

Enviopack.description: Enviopack is a technological solution designed to optimize ecommerce logistics by improving efficiency and streamlining operational processes.
Enviopack.history: Enviopack is an Argentine ecommerce logistics optimization platform designed to help online sellers manage shipments across multiple carriers. The platform integrates with Argentine carriers and ecommerce platforms to streamline the packing and shipping process for SMB merchants.
Enviopack.summary_abuse: Enviopack is a logistics management platform for Argentine ecommerce sellers. No notable abuse signal. The platform is used by merchants for shipping management and does not offer features that are commonly exploited for fraud.

Envo eCommerce.description: Envo eCommerce is a fast, clean and modern-looking responsive free WooCommerce theme for WordPress.
Envo eCommerce.history: Envo eCommerce is a free responsive WordPress WooCommerce theme from Envo Themes, part of the same family as Envo Shop and Envo Storefront. The theme is distributed through the WordPress theme directory under GPL license.
Envo eCommerce.summary_abuse: Envo eCommerce is a free WordPress WooCommerce theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer theme template with no hosted service surface.

Envo Shop.description: Envo Shop is a fast, clean and modern-looking responsive free WooCommerce WordPress theme by Envo Themes.
Envo Shop.history: Envo Shop is a free WordPress WooCommerce theme created by Envo Themes, a commercial theme developer. It is part of a family of free WooCommerce themes (Envo Storefront, Envo eCommerce) released by the same developer. The theme is distributed on the WordPress theme repository under GPL license.
Envo Shop.summary_abuse: Envo Shop is a free WordPress WooCommerce theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a theme used to build online stores and has no direct consumer-facing service that could be exploited.

Envo Storefront.description: Envo Storefront is a fast, clean and modern-looking responsive WooCommerce theme for WordPress.
Envo Storefront.history: Envo Storefront is a free responsive WordPress WooCommerce theme from Envo Themes, part of a family of similar free ecommerce themes including Envo Shop and Envo eCommerce. The theme is distributed through the WordPress theme directory under the GPL license.
Envo Storefront.summary_abuse: Envo Storefront is a free WordPress WooCommerce theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer theme with no hosted service surface.

Envoke.description: Envoke is a CASL-compliant email marketing software designed for communications professionals in business, education, and the public sector, with a focus on security and compliance.
Envoke.history: Envoke is a Canadian email marketing platform specifically designed for CASL compliance (Canada's Anti-Spam Legislation). The company stores all data in Canada (Toronto and Montreal data centers) and bills in CAD. It serves municipalities, universities, healthcare organizations, and other Canadian entities requiring stringent email consent management.
Envoke.summary_abuse: Envoke is a CASL-compliant email marketing platform focused on the Canadian market. Its compliance-first design, data residency in Canada, and consent management features make it less susceptible to being used for spam than generic email platforms. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Envolve Tech.description: Envolve Tech is a platform that provides virtual shopping assistants designed to guide customers through online purchases.
Envolve Tech.history: Envolve Tech is a small platform providing virtual shopping assistant tools for ecommerce websites. The company is independently operated and niche in scale. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Envolve Tech.summary_abuse: Envolve Tech is a virtual shopping assistant platform with no notable abuse signal. The service is used by small ecommerce sites to improve visitor engagement.

Envoy.description: Envoy is an open-source edge and service proxy, designed for cloud-native applications.
Envoy.history: Envoy proxy was originally built at Lyft in 2016 as an internal service mesh and edge proxy. Lyft open-sourced it in September 2016, and it was donated to the CNCF in September 2017. Envoy was the third CNCF project to graduate (after Kubernetes and Prometheus) in November 2018. It is the foundational data plane for Istio and many service mesh implementations, used in production by Google, Microsoft, Netflix, Stripe, Airbnb, and hundreds of other companies.
Envoy.summary_abuse: Envoy is a foundational cloud-native infrastructure proxy with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is used for critical production traffic in major enterprise environments and cloud-native deployments worldwide.

Envybox.description: Envybox is a multiservice for increasing sales.
Envybox.history: Envybox is a Russian SaaS platform offering website sales conversion tools including callback widgets, chat, lead forms, and CRM features for small businesses. The company is based in Russia and primarily serves the Russian-speaking market.
Envybox.summary_abuse: Envybox is a Russian website conversion tool platform. The service provides callback request widgets and lead capture forms. As a Russian-based platform with limited transparency, it presents moderate privacy concerns. The callback and lead collection features have potential for data collection abuse without adequate consent mechanisms.

Enyo.description: Enyo is an open-source JavaScript framework for cross-platform for mobile, desktop, TV and web applications.
Enyo.history: Enyo was originally developed by Palm and HP as the JavaScript framework for webOS devices. After HP acquired Palm in 2010 and abandoned the consumer webOS platform in 2011, the framework was open-sourced. It was used for cross-platform mobile and desktop development. The project has been largely abandoned since HP wound down webOS development.
Enyo.summary_abuse: Enyo is an abandoned JavaScript framework with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It was originally developed for webOS and is no longer in active development.

Enzuzo.description: Enzuzo is a data privacy management solution that helps organizations manage consent, comply with privacy regulations, and maintain control over personal data across digital platforms.
Enzuzo.history: Enzuzo was founded around 2019 in Canada as a data privacy management platform. The company helps organizations manage consent, privacy policies, cookie banners, and compliance with GDPR, CCPA, and PIPEDA. It serves small and medium businesses and ecommerce platforms with privacy compliance tooling.
Enzuzo.summary_abuse: Enzuzo is a data privacy compliance platform designed to help businesses manage consent and privacy policies. No notable abuse signal. The platform itself is a compliance tool focused on protecting user privacy.

eola.description: eola is a booking platform that enables users to manage reservations, schedules, and availability for services or events through a centralized online system.
eola.history: eola is a UK-based booking and scheduling management platform for activity and experience businesses; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
eola.summary_abuse: eola is an activity booking platform with no notable abuse signal; its niche focus on activity businesses limits fraud exposure.

ePages.description: ePages is a provider of cloud-based online shop solutions.
ePages.history: ePages traces its roots to 1997 when Intershop launched Intershop ePages as a hosting platform for small businesses. After the dot-com bust, co-founder Wilfried Beeck acquired the small-business software rights in 2002 and renamed the company ePages, headquartered in Hamburg, Germany. The company has since grown to serve over 100,000 merchants primarily through hosting provider partnerships across Europe.
ePages.summary_abuse: ePages is a cloud ecommerce platform for small businesses with no notable abuse signal. It hosts legitimate merchant stores through web hosting provider partnerships. Like any ecommerce platform it could be used to set up fraudulent storefronts, but ePages enforces terms of service and is not a platform known for enabling scam or phishing activity.

Epicor.description: Epicor is an enterprise resource planning software suite built to support manufacturing, distribution, retail, and service industries.
Epicor.history: Epicor Software Corporation was founded in 1972 in Austin, Texas and has grown to become a major ERP software provider for manufacturing, distribution, retail, and services industries. The company has undergone several ownership changes over its 50-year history, including private equity ownership. In 2024, Epicor was named a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud ERP for Product-Centric Enterprises for the second consecutive year.
Epicor.summary_abuse: Epicor is an enterprise ERP platform used by manufacturers, distributors, and retailers. No notable abuse signal. The platform is deployed in controlled enterprise environments with no consumer-facing features.

Eploy.description: Eploy is an end-to-end multilingual e-recruitment platform that helps to manage the entire recruitment process from job requisition through to onboarding.
Eploy.history: Eploy is a UK-based end-to-end multilingual e-recruitment platform founded around 2000. The company offers applicant tracking, onboarding, and recruitment management tools for UK and international businesses. The platform is independently owned and operates primarily in the UK recruitment technology market.
Eploy.summary_abuse: Eploy is a UK recruitment platform used by HR departments for applicant tracking and onboarding. No notable abuse signal. The platform handles candidate personal data in a regulated employment context.

Epoch.history: Epoch is a general-purpose real-time JavaScript charting library originally created at Fastly in 2014 and open-sourced. It provides both static reporting charts (built on D3.js) and high-performance real-time charts (using HTML5 Canvas). The project has not seen active development since around 2016 and is effectively abandoned.
Epoch.summary_abuse: Epoch is an open source JavaScript charting library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Epom.history: Epom was founded in 2010 as an independent ad technology company offering ad server and DSP (demand-side platform) solutions. Based in Ukraine with 350+ clients across 40+ countries, the platform serves publishers, advertisers, and ad networks for programmatic and direct digital advertising management.
Epom.summary_abuse: Epom is a digital advertising platform offering ad serving infrastructure. Ad tech platforms are regularly associated with malvertising, where malicious ads are injected into legitimate ad networks. Epom's self-serve ad network features and programmatic buying capabilities create surfaces where abusive or deceptive ads can be served. Like most ad tech platforms, its legitimacy-to-abuse ratio depends on customer vetting practices.

Epoq.description: Epoq is a platform that leverages artificial intelligence to deliver one-to-one personalization in e-commerce, enhancing product recommendations and user interactions through data-driven insights.
Epoq.history: Epoq is a German ecommerce AI personalization and recommendation engine company. The company developed AI-powered product recommendation technology for online shops. Epoq became part of Entirely, an open MarTech ecosystem that combines solutions from Censhare, Facelift, Marmind, Elaine, and Epoq into a single integrated platform.
Epoq.summary_abuse: Epoq is a B2B ecommerce personalization platform used by online retailers. No notable abuse signal. The platform provides recommendation engine and personalization features to established merchants.

EPrints.history: EPrints was developed at the University of Southampton School of Electronics and Computer Science and first released around 2000. It is a free and open-source institutional repository platform supporting OAI-PMH metadata harvesting, released under GPL-3.0. The software has been widely deployed in academic institutions worldwide for managing open access research publications, data, and theses.
EPrints.summary_abuse: EPrints is a legitimate academic institutional repository software developed by the University of Southampton with no known abuse incidents. The software is deployed in academic institutions for open-access archiving and has no consumer-facing surface that would enable abuse.

ePublishing.description: ePublishing is a media tech stack that enhances revenue, productivity, and engagement through AI-powered solutions, SEO dominance, and audience integration.
ePublishing.history: ePublishing is a US-based media technology platform for publishers, providing CMS, audience management, and revenue tools; founded in the late 1990s to serve trade and B2B media companies.
ePublishing.summary_abuse: ePublishing is a B2B media technology platform serving trade publishers with no notable abuse signal.

EqualWeb.description: EqualWeb provides a web accessibility overlay, and helps some people with disabilities access digital information.
EqualWeb.history: EqualWeb is an Israeli web accessibility platform providing ADA and WCAG compliance solutions. The company is recognized by W3C for WCAG 2.2 compliance and offers AI-powered accessibility widgets, auditing, and managed compliance services. It serves thousands of businesses seeking to comply with ADA, AODA, and EU EN 301 549 accessibility standards.
EqualWeb.summary_abuse: EqualWeb is a web accessibility compliance platform. No notable abuse signal. The platform injects accessibility overlay widgets on client websites, which some accessibility advocates have criticized as insufficient substitutes for genuine accessibility improvements, but this is a technical concern rather than fraud or malicious use.

EraofEcom Cartroids.description: EraofEcom Cartroids makes it easy for you to create highly targeted upsells, cross-sells and bundle offers.
EraofEcom Cartroids.history: EraofEcom Cartroids is a Shopify upsell and cross-sell app developed by the EraofEcom brand. The product is part of a suite of Shopify conversion apps created by EraofEcom. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
EraofEcom Cartroids.summary_abuse: EraofEcom Cartroids is a Shopify upsell app. Upsell and cross-sell tools can employ dark patterns like pre-selected add-ons or high-pressure countdown timers. As a small Shopify app, it has limited abuse surface beyond the manipulative UX patterns common in the upsell app category.

EraofEcom MTL.description: EraofEcom MTL is a Shopify pop up app that enables you to catch your website visitors.
EraofEcom MTL.history: EraofEcom MTL (Milk The Leads) is a Shopify pop-up and lead capture app developed by the EraofEcom brand. It is part of a suite of Shopify conversion optimization apps. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
EraofEcom MTL.summary_abuse: EraofEcom MTL is a Shopify pop-up lead capture app. Pop-up tools can collect email addresses with aggressive or misleading consent overlays. As a small conversion optimization app, its abuse potential is limited to the dark pattern risks inherent in pop-up marketing tools.

EraofEcom WinAds.description: EraofEcom WinAds is an all-in-one Facebook pixel app for Shopify.
EraofEcom WinAds.history: EraofEcom WinAds is a Facebook pixel management and ad tracking app for Shopify stores, part of the EraofEcom suite of conversion tools. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
EraofEcom WinAds.summary_abuse: EraofEcom WinAds is a Facebook pixel Shopify app. Facebook pixel apps enable detailed behavioral tracking of website visitors for ad retargeting. While this is legitimate ad tech, it raises privacy concerns regarding comprehensive user tracking without explicit consent.

eRate.description: eRate is a provider of digital advertising tools and inventory within global ad networks.
eRate.history: eRate is a digital advertising tools and inventory platform operating within global ad networks; no founding year or notable ownership history found in public records.
eRate.summary_abuse: eRate is an ad-network inventory platform. Ad-tech adjacent platforms carry inherent risk of being used for malvertising or tracking without consent, but no specific documented abuse has been reported for eRate directly.

Erlang.description: Erlang is a general-purpose, concurrent, functional programming language, and a garbage-collected runtime system.
Erlang.history: Erlang was developed at Ericsson in 1986 by Joe Armstrong, Robert Virding, and Mike Williams to power large-scale fault-tolerant telecommunications systems. Originally proprietary, Ericsson open-sourced it in 1998 under a permissive license. It powers Ericsson's AXD301 ATM switch with over 1.6 million lines of code. The language influenced modern concurrency models and is widely used in telecom, messaging systems (WhatsApp, RabbitMQ), and databases (CouchDB, Riak).
Erlang.summary_abuse: Erlang is a foundational programming language with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is used for critical distributed infrastructure including telecom systems, messaging platforms, and databases.

ERPNext.description: ERPNext is a free and open-source integrated Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software developed by Frappe Technologies.
ERPNext.history: ERPNext was initiated in 2008 by Rushabh Mehta at Frappe Technologies Pvt. Ltd., founded when Mehta needed to build an ERP system for his family business. Named ERPNext in 2010, it launched as a SaaS product before open-sourcing under GPL-3.0. Built on the Frappe Python framework with MariaDB, it has grown to become the world's most popular 100% open-source ERP, serving businesses from startups to large enterprises globally.
ERPNext.summary_abuse: ERPNext is a legitimate open-source ERP platform maintained by Frappe Technologies with no known abuse incidents. The software is widely deployed by businesses globally for legitimate enterprise operations. As a self-hosted solution, security depends on how operators manage their deployments. The SaaS cloud offering by Frappe is GDPR-compliant.

Errorception.description: Errorception is a error reporting service for client-side in-browser JavaScript errors.
Errorception.history: Errorception was a lightweight JavaScript error monitoring service created by one developer, Rakesh Pai, around 2011. It provided browser-side JavaScript error tracking and grouping. The service was shut down as of June 2024 after serving developers for over a decade.
Errorception.summary_abuse: Errorception was a JavaScript error monitoring service with no notable abuse signal. The service collected browser error data for developers and has been shut down as of 2024.

Erxes.description: Erxes is a platform that provides tools for marketing, sales, and customer service management.
Erxes.history: Erxes was founded in Mongolia around 2017 as an open-source experience operating system combining CRM, marketing automation, customer support, and team collaboration. The platform is written in TypeScript with React frontend and Apollo GraphQL backend, and is positioned as an open-source alternative to HubSpot, Zendesk, and other enterprise customer platforms.
Erxes.summary_abuse: Erxes is a self-hostable open source CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer-focused infrastructure tool for managing customer relationships, and its open source nature means it runs on customer infrastructure.

eShopCRM.description: eShopCRM is an ecommerce CRM for Shopify.
eShopCRM.history: eShopCRM is a CRM solution built specifically for Shopify merchants; no notable founding year or ownership changes found in public records.
eShopCRM.summary_abuse: eShopCRM is a Shopify-specific CRM tool. It has no consumer-facing hosting surface and no documented abuse signal.

EspoCRM.description: EspoCRM is an open-source CRM software solution designed for businesses of all sizes.
EspoCRM.history: EspoCRM is an open-source CRM application founded around 2014, with PHP backend and a single-page React-like frontend. The project is maintained by a Ukrainian team and distributed under AGPLv3 with a commercial license option. It supports contacts, leads, accounts, opportunities, email campaigns, and workflow automation, and has grown to be a well-regarded self-hosted CRM alternative.
EspoCRM.summary_abuse: EspoCRM is a self-hosted open source CRM with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted application, users control their own data and deployment. No consumer-facing hosted service exposes it to significant third-party abuse.

eSputnik.description: eSputnik is a marketing automation service for ecommerce.
eSputnik.history: eSputnik is a Ukrainian marketing automation and email/SMS platform founded around 2012, focused primarily on e-commerce businesses in Eastern Europe and beyond; no notable ownership changes are documented.
eSputnik.summary_abuse: eSputnik is a marketing automation platform with email and SMS sending capabilities; like all bulk-messaging tools it carries some risk of being used for spam, but no specific major abuse incidents are documented.

Essent SiteBuilder Pro.description: Essent SiteBuilder Pro is a fully-integrated web-based website design system, content management and ecommerce system.
Essent SiteBuilder Pro.history: Essent SiteBuilder Pro is a web-based website design system developed by Essent, a US-based software company. The product provides hosting customers with an integrated website building tool. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Essent SiteBuilder Pro.summary_abuse: Essent SiteBuilder Pro is a web-based site building tool for hosting customers. No notable abuse signal. The platform is used by small businesses to build basic websites.

eSSENTIAL Accessibility.description: eSSENTIAL Accessibility is a digital accessibility-as-a-service platform.
eSSENTIAL Accessibility.history: eSSENTIAL Accessibility is a Canadian digital accessibility-as-a-service platform providing WCAG compliance testing and assistive technology tools for enterprise clients; it has grown alongside increased regulatory focus on ADA and AODA compliance.
eSSENTIAL Accessibility.summary_abuse: eSSENTIAL Accessibility is a digital accessibility compliance service with no notable abuse signal; it operates in a compliance-focused enterprise market without public-facing content hosting features.

Essential Addons for Elementor.description: Essential Addons for Elementor gives you 70+ creative elements and extensions to help you extend the stock features of Elementor page builder.
Essential Addons for Elementor.history: Essential Addons for Elementor was launched in 2017 by WPDeveloper, a Bangladeshi WordPress product company. It grew to become the most popular Elementor addon with over 2 million active installs. WPDeveloper is also the company behind BetterDocs, NotificationX, and other popular WordPress plugins. The plugin has grown to offer 110+ widgets and maintained consistent security updates.
Essential Addons for Elementor.summary_abuse: Essential Addons for Elementor is a mainstream WordPress plugin with no notable abuse signal. Several CVEs have been disclosed and patched over its history, as is typical for widely-used WordPress plugins. The WPDeveloper team has maintained a reasonable patching cadence.

Essential Blocks.description: Essential Blocks is a Gutenberg Blocks page builder plugin that adds extra functionality to the WordPress Block Editor.
Essential Blocks.history: Essential Blocks is a Gutenberg block library plugin developed by WPDeveloper, the same Bangladesh-based company behind Essential Addons for Elementor. Launched around 2021 to target the growing Gutenberg editor ecosystem, it offers extra block types and functionality for WordPress's native block editor.
Essential Blocks.summary_abuse: Essential Blocks is a WordPress Gutenberg plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side template library with no hosted service surface.

Essential JS 2.history: Essential JS 2 is Syncfusion's modern JavaScript UI component library, developed by Syncfusion Inc., founded in 2001 in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina. Syncfusion is one of the leading UI component vendors for .NET and JavaScript developers. Essential JS 2 replaced the older Essential JS 1 library with a TypeScript-first, modular architecture supporting React, Angular, Vue, and plain JavaScript.
Essential JS 2.summary_abuse: Essential JS 2 is a JavaScript UI component library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is a licensed commercial toolkit used by enterprise development teams to build web applications.

Estate Track.description: Estate Track is an all-in-one website platform for estate agents that facilitates automated marketing and lead generation.
Estate Track.history: Estate Track is a UK-based all-in-one website platform for estate agents, providing property listing management, lead capture, and CRM tools. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Estate Track.summary_abuse: Estate Track is a UK estate agent website platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves real estate agencies for property listings and lead management.

Estore Compare.description: Estore Compare is a website optimisation software that offers A/B testing, CVR and LTV measuring tools.
Estore Compare.history: Estore Compare is an A/B testing and conversion rate optimization tool offered by Estore Corporation, a Japanese e-commerce solutions company founded in 1999 and headquartered in Minato-ku, Tokyo. Estore Corporation provides integrated e-commerce platform services primarily to Japanese online retailers, and Estore Compare is one of its optimization products.
