Estore Compare.summary_abuse: Estore Compare has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B A/B testing tool for Japanese e-commerce merchants, deployed as a script on merchant-owned websites. No consumer-facing features or anonymous signup are present.

Estore Shopserve.description: Estore Shopserve is an all-in-one payment processing and ecommerce solution.
Estore Shopserve.history: Estore Shopserve is an all-in-one ecommerce and payment processing solution from Japanese company eStore. It serves Japanese merchants with integrated payment and store management capabilities. eStore is one of the established Japanese ecommerce platform providers.
Estore Shopserve.summary_abuse: Estore Shopserve is a Japanese ecommerce and payment processing platform. As with any ecommerce platform, there is limited exposure to fraudulent storefronts. The platform operates in the Japanese market with payment integration that provides some KYC barriers.

ESW.description: ESW (eShopWorld) is a company providing payments, shipping, and delivery services focusing on cross-border ecommerce.
ESW.history: ESW (eShopWorld) is an Irish cross-border e-commerce technology company providing payment processing, shipping, and compliance services for global DTC brands. The platform operates across 200 markets and partners with Shopify and Adyen. ESW enables enterprise brands to launch localized direct-to-consumer storefronts internationally, handling duties, taxes, fraud protection, and currency conversion.
ESW.summary_abuse: ESW is a legitimate enterprise cross-border e-commerce infrastructure provider with no known abuse incidents. The company is GDPR-compliant and PCI DSS certified, operating as a payment processor for major global brands. No security incidents or fraudulent use have been publicly reported.

eSyndiCat.history: eSyndiCat is a PHP/MySQL directory software developed by Intelliants LLC, a web development company. It offers both a free limited version and a paid premium edition and has been available since the mid-2000s.
eSyndiCat.summary_abuse: eSyndiCat is a self-hosted PHP directory software. Directory software can be used to build link farms, which are sometimes used for SEO manipulation, but eSyndiCat itself has no notable malware hosting or phishing abuse record. No specific security incidents have been publicly documented.

Etail Systems.description: Etail Systems is an all-in-one centralized platform to manage all of your ecommerce operations.
Etail Systems.history: Etail Systems is a US-based centralized ecommerce management platform that helps multichannel retailers manage inventory, orders, and listings across multiple marketplaces and channels. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Etail Systems.summary_abuse: Etail Systems is a B2B ecommerce management platform for multichannel retailers. No notable abuse signal. The platform is used by established merchants for inventory and order management.

Etch.description: Etch is a developer-focused visual development environment for WordPress that combines WYSIWYG editing with IDE-level control and full block editor compatibility.
Etch.history: Etch is a developer-focused visual development environment for WordPress that combines visual editing with code. The product targets WordPress developers who want visual tooling while maintaining code control. No notable ownership changes found.
Etch.summary_abuse: Etch is a developer WordPress tooling product with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted development tool used by WordPress developers.

Etermio.description: Etermio is an automated system for online appointment scheduling.
Etermio.history: Etermio is an automated online appointment scheduling system primarily serving healthcare and service-oriented businesses. Pricing starts from approximately 29 EUR per month. No notable ownership changes found.
Etermio.summary_abuse: Etermio is an appointment scheduling SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by healthcare and service businesses for managing bookings.

Etherpad.description: Etherpad is an open-source, web-based collaborative real-time editor, allowing authors to simultaneously edit a text document, and see all of the participants' edits in real-time, with the ability to display each author's text in their own colour.
Etherpad.history: Etherpad was launched in November 2008 as a collaborative real-time document editor. Google acquired the company in December 2009 and released the source code under Apache License 2.0 on December 17, 2009, which became the basis for Google Wave and Google Docs collaborative features. The open-source project has since been maintained by an independent community and is used by Wikimedia Foundation, EU public institutions, and universities worldwide.
Etherpad.summary_abuse: Etherpad is an open-source collaborative editor commonly deployed as a public service. Public Etherpad instances have occasionally been used to host spam, phishing content, or malicious links, as anonymous document creation is typically allowed. Self-hosted deployments have full control over this. No structural abuse with the software itself.

Ethers.description: Ethers is a complete, tiny and simple Ethereum library.
Ethers.history: Ethers.js is a complete, compact, and simple Ethereum library created by Richard Moore (ricmoo) around 2016. It emerged as an alternative to Web3.js and became the most widely adopted JavaScript library for interacting with the Ethereum blockchain. The library is used in the majority of Ethereum dApps, wallets, and Web3 tooling.
Ethers.summary_abuse: Ethers.js is a blockchain interaction library used by Ethereum applications. It has no abuse signal as a library, though it is used in both legitimate DeFi applications and malicious smart contracts or phishing dApps. The library itself is neutral infrastructure.

EthicalAds.description: EthicalAds is a privacy-preserving ad network targeting developers.
EthicalAds.history: EthicalAds was created around 2020 as a privacy-preserving ad network specifically targeting developers, spun out from the Read the Docs documentation hosting platform. The network uses content-based targeting instead of user tracking and serves ads to developer-focused websites and documentation. It is one of the few ad networks explicitly designed around GDPR compliance with no user tracking.
EthicalAds.summary_abuse: EthicalAds is a privacy-preserving developer-focused ad network with no notable abuse signal. The network's explicit privacy stance and content-based targeting approach, combined with its focus on technical documentation sites, makes it one of the least abuse-prone advertising platforms.

Eticex.description: Eticex is as an ecommerce infrastructure provider that offers ecommerce packages and customisable high-performance ecommerce solutions.
Eticex.history: Eticex is an ecommerce infrastructure provider offering packaged ecommerce solutions for small and medium businesses. The platform appears to be based in Turkey and serves the Turkish market. No notable ownership changes or major events found.
Eticex.summary_abuse: Eticex is a small ecommerce infrastructure provider. As with any ecommerce platform, it has some exposure to fraudulent storefronts if seller verification is weak. No documented abuse incidents found.

etika.description: etika is a fintech company based in Manchester which provide buy now pay later solution.
etika.history: etika is a Manchester-based fintech company providing buy now, pay later (BNPL) solutions for online and in-store purchases, founded around 2018.
etika.summary_abuse: etika is a BNPL fintech with exposure to identity fraud typical of the sector. BNPL services are a documented target for synthetic identity fraud and account takeover. Etika-specific abuse incidents are not widely documented.

Etix.description: Etix is an international web-based ticketing service provider for the entertainment, travel, and sports industries.
Etix.history: Etix was established in 2000 in Raleigh, North Carolina as an independent web-based ticketing platform for entertainment venues. The company grew to process over 50 million tickets per year for 4,100+ clients in 40 countries, becoming the largest independent ticketing company in North America. It has offices in the US, Germany, Netherlands, Austria, Japan, and China.
Etix.summary_abuse: Etix is an independent ticketing platform for entertainment venues. Ticketing platforms are occasionally targeted by scalpers using automated purchasing bots, and fake ticket scams can reference legitimate ticketing services. Etix has no documented major abuse incidents specific to fraud or malware. Its consumer-facing ticketing pages are an indirect fraud vector through third-party scalping.

Etracker.description: Etracker is a web optimisation solution.
Etracker.history: Etracker was founded around 2000 in Hamburg, Germany, as a web analytics solution focused on GDPR compliance and data privacy. It positioned itself as a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics, processing all data on German servers. In December 2025, Etracker was acquired by JustRelate, a German CRM and CX software company.
Etracker.summary_abuse: Etracker is a privacy-centric web analytics platform with minimal abuse risk. It is designed to track website visitor behavior for legitimate site owners, with no anonymous signup feature that would enable mass abuse. As a paid B2B service targeting European businesses concerned with GDPR compliance, its customer base is vetted. There are no documented cases of Etracker infrastructure being abused for tracking without consent or for malicious purposes. The platform's strong GDPR posture (consent-free tracking mode compliant with TDDDG, ePrivacyseal certified) makes it a legally safe choice rather than a privacy threat.

eTrigue.description: eTrigue is a demand generation and sales acceleration software that helps businesses identify, engage, and convert prospects through automated marketing and lead nurturing.
eTrigue.history: eTrigue is a US-based B2B demand generation and marketing automation platform founded around 2004, targeting small and mid-market companies; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are publicly documented.
eTrigue.summary_abuse: eTrigue is a B2B marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal; its enterprise positioning and lead-nurturing focus do not expose it to common fraud vectors.

eucookie.eu.history: eucookie.eu is a cookie consent and EU cookie law compliance tool for websites; it appears to be a small independent project that may no longer be actively maintained, with the domain potentially for sale.
eucookie.eu.summary_abuse: eucookie.eu is a cookie consent banner tool. It has no consumer-facing content hosting surface and no documented abuse signal.

Eveho.description: Eveho is a platform that enables the creation of digital dealerships by providing tools to set up and manage online vehicle sales.
Eveho.history: Eveho was founded in 2020 in Bordeaux, France, by Armand Faure and Alban Combaud. The company offers a SaaS platform for used car dealers to create digital dealerships with e-commerce websites, photo and 360-degree imaging, BtoB marketplaces, and call tracking. It remains an early-stage, unfunded startup with around 8 employees as of 2024.
Eveho.summary_abuse: Eveho is a niche B2B SaaS platform for automotive dealers with no notable abuse signal. As a paid service for car dealerships, it does not offer anonymous or free-tier access that could be exploited by bad actors. There are no documented reports of Eveho infrastructure being used for fraud or malicious purposes.

Eventlink.description: Eventlink is a platform for managing events, offering tools for registration, communication, and collaboration in a unified interface.
Eventlink.history: Eventlink is a US-based SaaS platform developed to help athletic directors manage school sports programs, offering scheduling, registrations, athlete management, ticketing, and communications in one platform. The product emerged from a state athletic association and targets K-12 school sports administration. No notable ownership changes or controversies are documented.
Eventlink.summary_abuse: Eventlink is a niche B2B SaaS platform for school athletic administration with minimal abuse risk. It serves school districts and state athletic associations. There are no documented instances of Eventlink infrastructure being used for malicious purposes.

EventOn.description: EventON is event calendar for WordPress.
EventOn.history: EventON was first released on CodeCanyon in 2011 by developer Ashan Jay and has grown to become one of the best-selling WordPress event calendar plugins with 63,000+ paid installations. A free lite version is available on WordPress.org, with premium features sold through the developer's own site. No ownership changes or notable controversies are documented.
EventOn.summary_abuse: EventON is a locally-installed WordPress plugin with minimal abuse risk. Site owners control it directly and it has no cloud-hosted component that could be abused for phishing or spam. There are no notable reports of EventON being exploited for malicious purposes. Like any widely-distributed WordPress plugin, vulnerabilities may occasionally be disclosed, but the plugin has a reasonable maintenance track record.

Everflow.description: Everflow is a partner marketing analytics platform.
Everflow.history: Everflow was founded in Mountain View, California, by Kanwar Dhaliwal and Sam Darawish, who previously co-founded Moolah Media. The company launched the Everflow partner marketing platform to serve brands, agencies, and ad networks for affiliate and performance marketing. It grew to 1,000+ clients including J.G. Wentworth, Riot Games, and ClassPass. No ownership changes are documented; the company remains independently owned.
Everflow.summary_abuse: Everflow is an affiliate and partner marketing tracking platform. Affiliate marketing ecosystems have inherent abuse risks since they can be exploited by fraudulent affiliates generating fake clicks, leads, or installs to earn commissions. Everflow offers click fraud and conversion fraud detection tools to mitigate this. The platform itself is a paid B2B service not directly accessible by end users, but the networks it powers may be abused by bad-faith affiliates. Everflow is not implicated in any specific large-scale fraud incidents.

Evermos.description: Evermos is a connected commerce platform that enables resellers and drop shippers to earn commissions.
Evermos.history: Evermos was founded in November 2018 in Indonesia as a social commerce platform focused on reseller-driven sales of halal products. The platform grew rapidly to over 1.1 million active resellers (86% women) and 6,400+ UMKM (SMB) partners. It received recognition from Forbes Asia 100 to Watch and the UN Women 2022 Indonesian Women Empowerment Principles award. The company is VC-backed and positions itself as supporting women's economic empowerment in the Indonesian Muslim community.
Evermos.summary_abuse: Evermos is an Indonesian social commerce platform for resellers. As a free-to-join platform with a large, diverse user base, it could face abuse from fraudulent resellers or counterfeit product listings. However, the platform focuses on curated halal products from vetted UMKM partners, which reduces product fraud risk. There are no documented large-scale abuse incidents specific to Evermos.

Evernote.description: Evernote is a digital workspace that organizes notes, tasks, and schedules in one place for project management and information retention.
Evernote.history: Evernote was founded in 2007 by Stepan Pachikov and launched publicly in June 2008. It rapidly grew to 150 million users by 2015 and was considered a unicorn startup. However, the company struggled in the mid-2010s with leadership turnover, layoffs, and inability to compete with collaboration tools like Notion. In November 2022, Italian app developer Bending Spoons acquired Evernote. Following the acquisition, Bending Spoons laid off 129 Evernote employees in early 2023 and shifted the product toward a more restricted free tier with enhanced paid plans.
Evernote.summary_abuse: Evernote is primarily used for legitimate note-taking and productivity. The platform has had minor abuse exposure — notes can store arbitrary content, and the service's sync infrastructure has historically been exploited in isolated cases as a command-and-control channel in malware campaigns, though this was not widespread. Evernote processes user data including note contents and has a history of privacy incidents, including a 2013 data breach. After acquisition by Bending Spoons, the company significantly reduced its free tier, limiting large-scale abuse via free accounts.

Everviz.description: Everviz is an online tool for creating interactive charts and maps, enabling data visualization with customizable features.
Everviz.history: Everviz was rebranded from Highcharts Cloud on March 1, 2020, and operates as a separate subsidiary called Visual Elements. It is built on the well-known Highcharts charting library developed by Norwegian company Highsoft, and offers a no-code interface for creating interactive charts and data visualizations. The Norwegian/Scandinavian media industry is a primary target market. No ownership changes beyond the rebranding are documented.
Everviz.summary_abuse: Everviz is a data visualization tool used for creating and embedding charts. It has minimal abuse risk as it serves a professional audience of journalists and data analysts. There are no documented instances of Everviz being used for malicious purposes.

EverWeb.description: EverWeb is a website builder that allows users to create websites without any coding required.
EverWeb.history: EverWeb is a Mac and Windows website builder developed by RAGE Software, a Canadian company. It was created as a replacement for Apple's discontinued iWeb application, initially targeting Mac users who were already familiar with that product. RAGE Software had previously developed SEO tools for Mac. EverWeb added Windows support in later years. No ownership changes are documented.
EverWeb.summary_abuse: EverWeb is a downloadable website builder application with minimal abuse risk. Users download the desktop software to create and publish websites. There are no notable reports of EverWeb being used for malicious purposes. As a small niche desktop product with a vetted customer base of individual website owners, its attack surface for abuse is minimal.

EverWondr.description: EverWondr is a network providing a collection of plug-ins, including a calendar, directory, blog, guestbook, gallery, and survey.
EverWondr.history: EverWondr Network is a small US-based software company based in North Carolina that provides event calendar, directory, and community publishing plug-ins for websites. The platform targets local tourism boards and businesses to help them aggregate and share event content across partner sites. The company has remained small and independently operated with no documented ownership changes.
EverWondr.summary_abuse: EverWondr is a niche B2B event calendar and directory plugin provider targeting local tourism and small businesses. There are no documented reports of abuse or fraud involving EverWondr infrastructure.

EveryAction.description: EveryAction provides fundraising software, donor management software, and CRM software to nonprofit organisations.
EveryAction.history: EveryAction was founded in 2011 and became a leading CRM platform for nonprofits, combining fundraising, donor management, and advocacy tools. In 2021, EveryAction was acquired by and became part of Bonterra (formerly Social Solutions), a major nonprofit technology company. Bonterra consolidated several nonprofit software brands including Salsa Labs, GiveGab, and Mobilize under its umbrella. EveryAction now serves as the foundation of Bonterra's Fundraising and Engagement solution.
EveryAction.summary_abuse: EveryAction is a nonprofit CRM platform with minimal direct abuse risk. It is primarily used by legitimate nonprofits for donor management and fundraising campaigns. The platform processes significant volumes of donor personal data and financial contributions, making data security important. There are no documented large-scale abuse incidents specific to EveryAction infrastructure.

Eveve.description: Eveve is a restaurant table booking widget.
Eveve.history: Eveve was founded in 2007 and has grown into the world's largest independently owned restaurant reservation supplier, having seated over 20 million diners. Its TELOS booking platform is used by over 780 restaurants across 11 countries. Unlike competitors such as OpenTable and Resy, Eveve uses a flat licence fee model rather than per-diner charges. The company remains privately owned and profitable.
Eveve.summary_abuse: Eveve is a restaurant reservation platform used by restaurants and their customers. The risk of abuse is low, as the platform primarily handles table bookings for legitimate restaurants. There are no documented cases of Eveve infrastructure being used for fraud, spam, or other malicious purposes.

Evidence.description: Evidence is a real-time social proof tool designed to increase website conversions by displaying recent customer activity and engagement.
Evidence.history: Evidence was founded around 2021 by a Canadian team and launched as an open source business intelligence framework that uses SQL and markdown to build interactive data reports. The project received notable coverage on TechCrunch in 2023. It has grown a developer community and offers a cloud hosting platform alongside the self-hostable open source version. The project is backed by venture capital.
Evidence.summary_abuse: Evidence is an open source BI framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool for building internal data reports and dashboards. As a code-based tool that runs in data team environments, it has essentially no consumer-facing attack surface for phishing or spam abuse.

Evidon.description: Evidon is a transparency company that helps organizations educate consumers on how and why data is collected, as well as provide consumers with the ability to give and withdraw consent to their data being used.
Evidon.history: Evidon was founded in 2009 as The Better Advertising Project by Scott Meyer, originally to manage the Digital Advertising Alliance's AdChoices program. It acquired the Ghostery browser extension from David Cancel in January 2009. In January 2011, the company rebranded as Evidon. In February 2017, Evidon sold Ghostery to Cliqz GmbH to focus on enterprise B2B compliance tools. Also in 2017, Evidon launched the Universal Consent Platform and was acquired by Crownpeak. The Evidon brand was subsequently retired, with its technology absorbed into Crownpeak's product suite.
Evidon.summary_abuse: Evidon was a privacy and consent management technology provider with no notable abuse history. Its products were designed to help advertisers and publishers comply with privacy regulations such as GDPR, making it a privacy-positive technology rather than a threat. The Evidon brand was retired after acquisition by Crownpeak in 2017.

Eviivo.description: Eviivo is an online booking system that enables hosts and hoteliers to manage reservations and availability.
Eviivo.history: Eviivo is a UK-based property management system for hotels, B&Bs, vacation rentals, and other accommodation providers. Founded around 2007, the company developed its all-in-one eviivo Suite platform for managing reservations, OTA channels, payments, and guest communications. The platform serves over 30,000 properties globally. No major ownership changes are documented; the company remains independently operated.
Eviivo.summary_abuse: Eviivo is a property management and booking platform for hotels and vacation rentals with minimal abuse risk. The platform handles payment processing for bookings and connects to OTAs. There are no documented instances of Eviivo infrastructure being misused for fraud or other malicious purposes.

Evolok.description: Evolok is an engagement and monetization platform for digital media.
Evolok.history: Evolok is a UK-based digital subscription and paywall platform for online publishers. The company provides identity management, access control, and content monetisation tools for media organisations. Notable clients include The Telegraph, New Statesman, El Pais, and Indian Express. No major ownership changes are documented.
Evolok.summary_abuse: Evolok is a subscription and paywall platform used by digital publishers to control content access. It has minimal abuse risk as it is a B2B enterprise product with vetted media clients. There are no documented reports of Evolok infrastructure being exploited for malicious purposes.

Evolve Media.description: Evolve Media is a publishing platform that offers contextual environments for partners to connect and engage with millions of loyal readers through passionate writing and artful storytelling.
Evolve Media.history: Evolve Media was founded in 2001 by Brian Fitzgerald and Aaron Broder in Los Angeles. The company built a portfolio of premium enthusiast content websites targeting men and women, reaching over 78 million people globally. It operates proprietary advertising technology and offers publishers digital content development, video production, and advertising sales. The company remains privately held.
Evolve Media.summary_abuse: Evolve Media is a digital advertising network that embeds third-party tracking and advertising scripts on publisher websites. As an ad-tech company, its scripts may include heavy tracker payloads that follow users across sites for targeted advertising. There are no documented large-scale fraud incidents involving Evolve Media, but as an ad network, its infrastructure could be an abuse vector for malvertising if not carefully monitored.

Evvnt.description: Evvnt is a digital events marketing and management platform.
Evvnt.history: Evvnt is an event promotion and ticketing platform that distributes event listings to 50+ event discovery sites. The company powers event ticketing for media partners including The Seattle Times, Cumulus, and Lee Enterprises. With approximately 26 employees as of 2026, it remains a small independent company. No major ownership changes are documented.
Evvnt.summary_abuse: Evvnt is an event promotion distribution platform. As a platform that publishes event listings to third-party sites, it could be abused for spam event listings or fake events. The platform is primarily used by legitimate event organizers and media partners, and its paid tiers create a barrier to mass abuse. There are no specific documented abuse incidents.

eWAY Payments.description: eWAY is a global omnichannel payment provider. The company processes secure credit card payments for merchants. eWay works through eCommerce.
eWAY Payments.history: eWAY was founded in 1998 in Australia as one of the earliest online payment processors in the Asia-Pacific region. It was acquired by Global Payments, one of the largest payment technology companies, giving it significant stability and global backing.
eWAY Payments.summary_abuse: eWAY is a PCI-DSS certified payment gateway with fraud monitoring. As with all payment processors, it is exposed to card fraud and fraudulent merchant onboarding at levels proportional to its scale. Its acquisition by Global Payments provides enhanced anti-fraud tooling.

ewiz commerce.description: ewiz commerce is a B2B solution that integrates advanced eCommerce technology and AI to help manufacturers, distributors, and wholesalers streamline operations and improve ROI.
ewiz commerce.history: ewiz commerce is a B2B e-commerce platform using AI to help manufacturers, distributors, and wholesalers build and optimize online sales channels; no notable ownership changes are documented.
ewiz commerce.summary_abuse: ewiz commerce is a B2B e-commerce platform for manufacturers and distributors with no notable abuse signal.

EWWW Image Optimizer.description: EWWW Image Optimizer is an image optimisation WordPress plugin designed to improve the performance of your website.
EWWW Image Optimizer.history: EWWW Image Optimizer is a WordPress plugin for automatic image compression that can optimize images using local server tools (jpegtran, optipng, pngquant, gifsicle, cwebp) or via the EWWW.io cloud service. The plugin has over 1 million active installations and 4.7 star rating from 1,700+ reviews. It supports lossless and lossy compression, lazy loading, next-gen WebP and AVIF conversion.
EWWW Image Optimizer.summary_abuse: EWWW Image Optimizer is a legitimate WordPress image optimization plugin with no known abuse incidents. The plugin is widely used for performance optimization and is available on WordPress.org. No security vulnerabilities or malicious behavior have been reported.

EX.CO.description: EX.CO (formerly Playbuzz) is an online publishing platform for publishers, brand agencies, and individual content creators to create content in interactive formats such as polls, quizzes, lists, video snippets, slideshows, and countdowns.
EX.CO.history: EX.CO was founded in 2013 in Israel. Originally called Playbuzz, the company rebranded to EX.CO in 2020. It is a machine-learning video and interactive content platform targeting media publishers, enabling video delivery, monetization, and AI-driven content recommendations across web, mobile, CTV, and DOOH. In 2021 the company acquired video ad tech firm Cedato to expand AI capabilities.
EX.CO.summary_abuse: EX.CO is a legitimate video and interactive content platform for publishers, but as an ad-tech platform it embeds heavy tracking and advertising technology on publisher pages. The platform serves video ads and collects extensive user behavioral data for targeting. This is inherent to its ad monetization business model rather than malicious abuse.

ExactMetrics.description: ExactMetrics (formerly Google Analytics Dashboard for WP) plugin helps you properly setup all the powerful Google Analytics tracking features without writing any code or hiring a developer.
ExactMetrics.history: ExactMetrics (formerly Google Analytics Dashboard for WP) is a WordPress plugin that integrates Google Analytics into WordPress dashboards. It is closely related to MonsterInsights and was originally developed as a simpler variant for site owners. The plugin provides free and paid tiers with pricing starting around $99.50/year. No major ownership changes are publicly documented.
ExactMetrics.summary_abuse: ExactMetrics is a WordPress analytics plugin that connects sites to Google Analytics. As it is locally installed on websites, there is no central abuse vector beyond standard Google Analytics data collection. There are no documented cases of ExactMetrics being specifically exploited for malicious purposes.

ExactVisitor.description: ExactVisitor is a tool that identifies and provides insights about individuals visiting a website.
ExactVisitor.history: Founded around 2014 by Demand Group in London, UK. ExactVisitor is a B2B visitor identification SaaS platform. No notable acquisitions, ownership changes, or deprecation events have been publicly documented.
ExactVisitor.summary_abuse: ExactVisitor is a B2B visitor identification tool that identifies companies and individuals visiting websites via a tracking pixel and reverse IP lookup. Its core function raises privacy considerations as it identifies website visitors without explicit consent, particularly relevant under GDPR. The vendor is UK-based and lists a compliance page and privacy policy. No notable public incidents of phishing, scam, or fraud abuse specifically tied to ExactVisitor have been documented. The tool is used by sales and marketing teams for lead generation rather than by bad actors, making its abuse profile low relative to consumer-facing tracking tools.

Excel Impact.description: Excel Impact is a provider of lead generation systems and advertiser solutions.
Excel Impact.history: Founded around 2014 as Excel Impact, LLC (branded as Excel) by Alex Matseikovich, Rodolfo Marrero, and Craig Sturgill. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, the company is a lead generation and performance marketing platform specializing in insurance verticals (Health, Medicare, Life, Auto, Home). Recognized as an Inc. 5000 honoree six times, ranked 16th on Deloitte Technology Fast 500 in 2017, and named EY Entrepreneur of the Year finalist in 2018. The company operates as an independent, privately held business serving insurance carriers and agencies.
Excel Impact.summary_abuse: Excel Impact is a lead generation SaaS platform focused on the US insurance market. The platform collects and sells consumer personal information including contact details, health indicators, financial data, and insurance history to insurance carriers, agencies, and ad networks. The insurance lead generation sector has documented abuse patterns including deceptive marketing, TCPA violations, and aggressive telemarketing practices. Excel Impact explicitly references TCPA, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM compliance and maintains a compliance team, indicating awareness of these risks. No specific abuse incidents involving Excel Impact were found in public sources, but the sector's nature makes abuse monitoring relevant.

Excentos.description: Excentos is a guided selling solution designed to assist customers in identifying and selecting specific products.
Excentos.history: Founded in 2007 by Ole Tangermann in Potsdam, Germany, as excentos Software GmbH, focused on guided selling solutions for e-commerce. The company raised a seed round in its founding year and has remained an independent SMB-scale SaaS vendor with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
Excentos.summary_abuse: Excentos is a B2B guided selling platform embedded into enterprise e-commerce sites; it has no notable abuse signal. The product assists legitimate retailers in product recommendation workflows and is not a consumer-facing hosting, email, or URL service that fraudsters typically exploit.

Exemptify.description: Exemptify allows you to conduct proper EU B2B transactions by validating EU VAT IDs.
Exemptify.history: Exemptify was created by daxanema and launched on the Shopify App Store on August 4, 2016. It is a niche EU VAT validation tool for Shopify merchants; no notable ownership changes or major events since launch.
Exemptify.summary_abuse: Exemptify is a narrow Shopify plugin for EU VAT ID validation and has no notable abuse signal. It does not host content, offer custom domains, or expose features attractive to fraudsters. Its sole function is tax-exemption logic within the Shopify checkout, limiting consumer-facing abuse surface substantially.

Exhibit.history: Exhibit is part of the SIMILE (Semantic Interoperability of Metadata and Information in unLike Environments) project developed at MIT, circa 2006. It is a JavaScript data-publishing framework for creating interactive data visualizations and faceted browsing on static web pages. The project has received no active maintenance for many years and is effectively abandoned.
Exhibit.summary_abuse: Exhibit has no abuse signal; it is an abandoned MIT academic JavaScript library for data visualization with no consumer-facing surface.

Exit Bee.description: Exit Bee is a platform that uses pattern recognition and machine learning to detect when users disengage from website content and are more likely to interact with ad campaigns.
Exit Bee.history: Exit Bee Limited is a London-based ad-tech company specializing in exit-intent advertising using pattern recognition and machine learning. The company provides a platform that detects when website users disengage from content to deliver targeted ad campaigns with high viewability. Privacy policy dates to May 2018 with GDPR compliance built in. The company participates in the IAB Europe Transparency and Consent Framework. No major ownership changes or controversies are publicly documented.
Exit Bee.summary_abuse: Exit Bee is an exit-intent advertising platform that deploys targeted ads when users are about to leave a website. As an ad-tech platform, it uses cookies and tracking technologies for behavioral advertising, collecting browsing data and serving targeted campaigns. The platform has the potential for misuse through deceptive ad formats or unwanted redirects common in the ad-tech sector. However, Exit Bee participates in the IAB Europe Transparency and Consent Framework and has a published GDPR-compliant privacy policy. No widely reported abuse incidents specific to Exit Bee were found.

ExitIntel.description: ExitIntel is a full service conversion optimisation agency that focuses on ecommerce companies.
ExitIntel.history: Founded 2019 as Exit Intelligence, LLC in Raleigh, NC; an email and SMS capture agency with proprietary SaaS platform serving ecommerce clients. No ownership changes or deprecation events on record.
ExitIntel.summary_abuse: ExitIntel has minimal abuse signal. It is a niche marketing SaaS providing email/SMS capture pop-ups for ecommerce merchants, operating as a managed agency service. No free tier, no anonymous signup, no custom content hosting — all factors that limit abuse surface. No documented abuse incidents or security research reports were found.

Exitshop.description: Exitshop is an ecommerce solution designed for managing and operating online stores.
Exitshop.history: Founded in 2014 by ExitShop s.r.o. in Prague, Czech Republic. A Czech e-commerce SaaS platform focused on mid-market online stores with multi-warehouse management and extensive third-party integrations. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events since founding.
Exitshop.summary_abuse: Exitshop has no notable abuse signal. It is a Czech-market SaaS e-commerce platform serving approximately 2,300+ online stores. Customers register with full business details (Czech business ID required), and the platform operates within a relatively controlled EU market. The platform does not offer anonymous signup or free-tier custom-domain hosting. As a hosted e-commerce solution, potential abuse vectors (fake storefronts, phishing) are mitigated by KYC through business registration and the small addressable market.

Exo Platform.description: Exo Platform is a social-collaboration software designed for enterprises.
Exo Platform.history: eXo Platform was founded in 2003 by Benjamin Mestrallet in Tunisia and France as an open-source enterprise social collaboration platform. The company has offered both a GPL-licensed community edition and a commercial enterprise edition throughout its history. It gained traction particularly in French-speaking markets and the public sector.
Exo Platform.summary_abuse: eXo Platform is an enterprise collaboration and social intranet platform with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B deployment tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

ExoClick.description: ExoClick is a Barcelona-based online advertising company, which provides online advertising services to both advertisers and publishers.
ExoClick.history: ExoClick was founded in 2006 in Barcelona by Benjamin Fonze. It grew into one of the largest global ad networks specializing in mainstream and adult traffic monetization. By 2025 it serves over 20 billion geo-targeted ads per day to 65,000 publisher platforms worldwide and employs about 106 people across 5 continents. In 2025 it introduced AI-based anti-adblock domain rotation and Native Video Thumbnails.
ExoClick.summary_abuse: ExoClick is heavily associated with adult and pop-under advertising networks and known for serving intrusive ad formats including push notifications and pop-unders. It actively circumvents ad blockers via automatic dynamic domain rotation every 3 days. Its focus on adult content verticals and aggressive ad formats raises significant concerns for user privacy and browsing experience.

experiencedCMS.history: experiencedCMS is a small open-source CMS developed by Berke Aras as a personal project, built on Twig templating and designed for blogs, personal sites, intranets, and business websites. The project is hosted on berkearas.de and targets a small niche userbase.
experiencedCMS.summary_abuse: experiencedCMS is a self-hosted open-source CMS built by a solo developer. It has no consumer-facing hosted surface and no documented abuse signal.

ExpertRec.description: ExpertRec is a collaborative Web search engine, which allows users share search histories through a web browser.
ExpertRec.history: ExpertRec was founded in 2014 in Bengaluru, India by Melchizedec Sundararaj and Seladurai Sundaraj. The company provides SaaS-based custom site search for online stores and websites. It has raised approximately $508K in funding from Oracle and Beenext. The platform has evolved to offer AI-powered semantic search, vector search, and multilingual search capabilities as of 2025.
ExpertRec.summary_abuse: ExpertRec has no known significant abuse history. It is a small B2B SaaS site search platform that processes website content to deliver search results. It collects search query analytics but does not engage in aggressive cross-site tracking or data brokering. No major privacy incidents or security breaches have been publicly reported.

Expivi.description: Expivi specialises in 3D visualisation technology for ecommerce stores.
Expivi.history: Founded in 2017 in Eindhoven, Netherlands, Expivi provides 3D product configurator and CPQ technology for ecommerce. Raised approximately $3.7M in seed funding in 2021 from ScaleNL, Rabobank and other Dutch investors. No major ownership changes since founding.
Expivi.summary_abuse: Expivi is a B2B SaaS 3D product configurator used by manufacturers and retailers. It has no notable abuse signal; the platform requires business sign-up and targets ecommerce integrations with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract phishing or fraud activity.

Exponea.description: Exponea is a cloud-based marketing analysis platform suitable for large and midsize organisations in a variety of industries.
Exponea.history: Founded in 2015 in Slovakia, Exponea was a customer data and experience platform (CDXP) combining CDP and marketing automation. It was acquired by Bloomreach in January 2021 when Bloomreach also secured $150M from Sixth Street, creating a combined ecommerce experience platform. Exponea was notably the world's first GDPR-certified SaaS company.
Exponea.summary_abuse: Exponea (now part of Bloomreach) is a B2B customer data and marketing automation platform targeting mid-market and enterprise ecommerce. As a backend analytics and CRM tool, it has minimal consumer-facing surface. No notable abuse incidents documented; marketing automation tools can be used for email spam but Exponea's enterprise focus and onboarding process reduce this risk.

Express.description: Express is a web application framework for Node.js, released as free and open-source software under the MIT License. It is designed for building web applications and APIs.
Express.history: Express.js was created by TJ Holowaychuk and first released in 2010 as an open-source minimal Node.js web framework inspired by Sinatra. It became the most popular Node.js framework and is now maintained under the OpenJS Foundation. Express v5.0 was released in late 2024 after years of development. It has over 67k GitHub stars, 43 million weekly npm downloads, and powers major platforms including Twitter and CodeSandbox.
Express.summary_abuse: Express.js has no known abuse history. It is a server-side framework that runs in controlled environments and does not collect user data or deploy tracking. As open source software under the MIT license, it is fully auditable. No significant security incidents or malicious packages have been associated with the canonical express npm package.

ExpressionEngine.description: ExpressionEngine is a free and open-source CMS.
ExpressionEngine.history: ExpressionEngine was created by EllisLab, founded in 2002, initially as pMachine Pro before evolving into ExpressionEngine. It was a paid CMS for many years until 2018 when EllisLab open-sourced it under the MIT license after 16 years. EllisLab was acquired by Digital Locations in 2019. The CMS lost significant market share to WordPress over the years but retains a niche user base.
ExpressionEngine.summary_abuse: ExpressionEngine is a self-hosted CMS with a long history and small but dedicated user base. Like any CMS, sites built on it can be compromised if unpatched, but the platform itself is not notably associated with abuse. Its small market share and self-hosted nature reduce its appeal for large-scale abuse campaigns.

Extend.description: Extend is a service that offers brands product and shipping protection, creating a new revenue source while enhancing customer loyalty and trust.
Extend.history: Founded in 2019 in San Francisco by Woody Levin, Extend offers API-based extended warranty and protection plan solutions for ecommerce merchants. It raised $260M in a Series C round in 2021 led by SoftBank Vision Fund 2 at a $1.6B valuation. In 2022 it became a fully licensed service contract provider across all 50 US states.
Extend.summary_abuse: Extend is a B2B warranty and protection plan SaaS targeted at ecommerce merchants. As an insurance-adjacent fintech with regulatory licensing requirements, it has minimal abuse surface. No notable phishing, fraud, or misuse incidents documented.

Extendify.description: Extendify is a popular platform used by WordPress site owners to create high-performing and highly-aesthetic websites with ease.
Extendify.history: Extendify is an AI-powered WordPress platform designed for hosting businesses to streamline WordPress onboarding. It creates websites using native WordPress block patterns without page builders. The product is embedded by web hosting companies as a white-label or co-branded service. Founded circa 2020.
Extendify.summary_abuse: Extendify is a WordPress onboarding and AI website creation tool targeted at hosting companies and their customers. No notable abuse profile; the platform is not directly consumer-facing and requires hosting account access to use.

ExtendThemes Calliope.description: ExtendThemes Calliope is an flexible, multipurpose WordPress child theme of Colibri WP.
ExtendThemes Calliope.history: ExtendThemes is a Romanian WordPress theme shop offering multipurpose drag-and-drop themes including Mesmerize, Materialis, Highlight, EmpowerWP, and Calliope. The themes are free on WordPress.org with premium add-ons. No major ownership changes documented.
ExtendThemes Calliope.summary_abuse: ExtendThemes produces free GPL-licensed WordPress themes distributed via WordPress.org. As a standard WordPress theme, it has no notable abuse profile. Potential risk is limited to sites built on these themes being vulnerable if the theme or plugins are unpatched.

ExtendThemes EmpowerWP.description: ExtendThemes EmpowerWP is an flexible, multipurpose WordPress theme.
ExtendThemes EmpowerWP.history: ExtendThemes is a Romanian WordPress theme shop offering multipurpose drag-and-drop themes including Mesmerize, Materialis, Highlight, EmpowerWP, and Calliope. The themes are free on WordPress.org with premium add-ons. No major ownership changes documented.
ExtendThemes EmpowerWP.summary_abuse: ExtendThemes produces free GPL-licensed WordPress themes distributed via WordPress.org. As a standard WordPress theme, it has no notable abuse profile. Potential risk is limited to sites built on these themes being vulnerable if the theme or plugins are unpatched.

ExtendThemes Highlight.description: ExtendThemes Highlight is an flexible, multipurpose WordPress theme.
ExtendThemes Highlight.history: ExtendThemes is a Romanian WordPress theme shop offering multipurpose drag-and-drop themes including Mesmerize, Materialis, Highlight, EmpowerWP, and Calliope. The themes are free on WordPress.org with premium add-ons. No major ownership changes documented.
ExtendThemes Highlight.summary_abuse: ExtendThemes produces free GPL-licensed WordPress themes distributed via WordPress.org. As a standard WordPress theme, it has no notable abuse profile. Potential risk is limited to sites built on these themes being vulnerable if the theme or plugins are unpatched.

ExtendThemes Materialis.description: ExtendThemes Materialis is an flexible, multipurpose WordPress theme.
ExtendThemes Materialis.history: ExtendThemes is a Romanian WordPress theme shop offering multipurpose drag-and-drop themes including Mesmerize, Materialis, Highlight, EmpowerWP, and Calliope. The themes are free on WordPress.org with premium add-ons. No major ownership changes documented.
ExtendThemes Materialis.summary_abuse: ExtendThemes produces free GPL-licensed WordPress themes distributed via WordPress.org. As a standard WordPress theme, it has no notable abuse profile. Potential risk is limited to sites built on these themes being vulnerable if the theme or plugins are unpatched.

ExtendThemes Mesmerize.description: ExtendThemes Mesmerize is an flexible, multipurpose WordPress theme.
ExtendThemes Mesmerize.history: ExtendThemes is a Romanian WordPress theme shop offering multipurpose drag-and-drop themes including Mesmerize, Materialis, Highlight, EmpowerWP, and Calliope. The themes are free on WordPress.org with premium add-ons. No major ownership changes documented.
ExtendThemes Mesmerize.summary_abuse: ExtendThemes produces free GPL-licensed WordPress themes distributed via WordPress.org. As a standard WordPress theme, it has no notable abuse profile. Potential risk is limited to sites built on these themes being vulnerable if the theme or plugins are unpatched.

ExtJS.description: ExtJS is a JavaScript framework developed by Sencha, designed for creating feature-rich, cross-platform web applications with a comprehensive set of UI components and tools.
ExtJS.history: Created by Jack Slocum in 2006-2007 as a YUI extension called YUI-ext, then rebranded as Ext JS 1.0. Sencha Inc was formed in 2010 via merger of the Ext JS team with JQTouch and Raphael. Sencha was acquired by IDERA in 2017. A Community Edition with revenue restrictions was announced in 2018. The framework is widely used in enterprise data-grid heavy applications.
ExtJS.summary_abuse: ExtJS is an enterprise JavaScript UI component framework used primarily in large internal business applications. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface and is not associated with phishing, malware, or scam activity. The framework's commercial licensing model and enterprise focus make it an unlikely vector for abuse.

Extole.description: Extole is an online marketing platform that enables brands and businesses to get new customers through loyalty and referral programs.
Extole.history: Extole was founded in 2009 in San Francisco by Brad Klaus and Andrew Beranbom as a referral and advocacy marketing platform. It has raised over $61 million in funding from investors including Redpoint Ventures and Norwest Venture Partners. The platform serves enterprise brands to run refer-a-friend and ambassador programs. In 2025, Extole launched a no-code Flow Builder for designing referral journeys and partnered with Jack Henry for financial services referral programs.
Extole.summary_abuse: Extole is a legitimate enterprise referral marketing platform with no significant abuse history. It collects customer behavioral and referral data to power marketing programs, which involves some privacy considerations around data sharing. The platform operates under a contractual enterprise model and adheres to applicable privacy laws. No significant data breaches or malicious behavior have been reported.

ExtraWatch.description: ExtraWatch is a tool that tracks clicks and helps optimize landing pages by providing actionable insights into user interactions.
ExtraWatch.history: ExtraWatch is a small web analytics plugin for WordPress and Joomla offering real-time visitor tracking, heatmaps, and conversion tracking. It has been available since around 2010 and is developed by a small independent team. No major ownership changes or controversies documented.
ExtraWatch.summary_abuse: ExtraWatch is a small analytics plugin for CMS platforms. It collects visitor data for site owners, which raises standard privacy considerations but has no documented abuse for phishing or fraud. As a niche plugin it has minimal exposure.

EyeFitU.description: EyeFitU is a Size-as-a-Service AI platform for ecommerce that provides automated sizing intelligence to improve fit accuracy and reduce returns.
EyeFitU.history: EyeFitU is an AI-powered size recommendation and virtual fitting technology company for fashion and footwear ecommerce. Founded around 2015 and based in Switzerland, it provides SizeFinder and SizeVisualizer tools that help shoppers find correct garment sizes to reduce returns. No major ownership changes documented.
EyeFitU.summary_abuse: EyeFitU is a B2B size recommendation SaaS embedded in fashion retail sites. The platform collects body measurement data from consumers which carries privacy implications, but it is not associated with phishing, fraud, or malware. No notable abuse incidents documented.

EyouCMS.description: Eyou CMS is a stable, open-source enterprise content management system built on the TP5.0 framework.
EyouCMS.history: EyouCMS is a free and open-source Chinese enterprise CMS built on ThinkPHP 5.0 framework. Developed by Kuaitui Technology, it targets Chinese small and medium businesses. Released circa 2016, it is one of the more popular Chinese CMS systems for business websites and supports WeChat mini programs via plugins.
EyouCMS.summary_abuse: EyouCMS is a Chinese open-source CMS primarily used by Chinese SME websites. As with any self-hosted CMS, poorly maintained installations are exploited for spam and SEO abuse. Several CVEs have been reported for SQL injection and XSS vulnerabilities. No organized abuse infrastructure documented but the platform has patchy security history typical of small-team PHP CMS projects.

eZ Publish.history: eZ Publish is an open-source CMS created by eZ Systems in Norway, first released in 1999. It evolved through multiple major versions and has been used for enterprise web publishing. The platform is now maintained under the Ibexa brand following a rebrand.
eZ Publish.summary_abuse: eZ Publish is a self-hosted CMS with no notable targeted abuse signal. Old unmaintained installations could be vulnerable to compromise, but it is not a platform specifically targeted by fraudsters.

eZCater.description: ezCater is a platform that enables businesses to order and manage office catering services from local and national restaurants.
eZCater.history: ezCater was founded in 2007 in Boston, MA as a marketplace connecting businesses with catering services; it raised significant VC funding and grew to become the leading corporate catering platform in the US.
eZCater.summary_abuse: ezCater is a corporate catering marketplace with no notable abuse signal; it connects businesses with food providers and has no consumer-facing content-hosting surface exploited by fraudsters.

EZLynx.description: EZLynx is a cloud-based software offering real-time comparative insurance rating, integrating with over 330 carriers for automated quoting and policy management​.
EZLynx.history: EZLynx is a US-based insurance agency management software platform providing a comparative rater, agency management system, and CRM integrated in one product. The platform connects to over 330 carriers and serves more than 37,000 independent insurance agencies. EZLynx is owned by Applied Systems, a leading insurance technology company.
EZLynx.summary_abuse: EZLynx is a legitimate insurance agency management software platform with no known abuse incidents. The platform is used exclusively by licensed insurance agencies and handles sensitive policy and client data. It operates in a regulated industry under applicable insurance laws.

Ezoic.description: Ezoic is a website optimisation platform for digital publishers and website owners powered by machine learning.
Ezoic.history: Founded in 2010 in Carlsbad, California by Dwayne Lafleur, Ezoic is an AI-driven ad revenue optimization platform for digital publishers. It is a Google Certified Publishing Partner and operates a Series B funded company. It accepts publishers at any traffic level making it accessible to small sites.
Ezoic.summary_abuse: Ezoic is an ad monetization network that injects third-party ad code on publisher websites, including heavy tracker and ad network integrations. While legitimately used by many publishers, the ad-tech nature means it ships multiple trackers per page. Low-quality content farms and MFA (Made For Advertising) sites frequently use Ezoic, which raises questions about ad quality and user experience. Ezoic participates in the Google ad ecosystem which provides some baseline anti-fraud controls.

EZproxy.description: EZproxy is a web server and a reverse proxy that is usually used by libraries as a reverse proxy in front of electronic educational resources databases (e.g.: Scopus, PubMed, or Web of Science) in order to provide authentication and protect privacy.
EZproxy.history: EZproxy is a web proxy server for libraries, originally developed by Chris Zagar and first released around 1999. It was acquired by OCLC (Online Computer Library Center) in 2008. The product is the dominant standard for remote access to library electronic resources globally, used by thousands of academic, public, and special libraries to authenticate patrons accessing licensed databases and e-resources from outside the library network.
EZproxy.summary_abuse: EZproxy is a legitimate library authentication proxy maintained by OCLC with no inherent abuse posture. However, credential stuffing attacks targeting library EZproxy installations have been documented since 2019, with attackers using compromised library credentials to harvest academic research content for redistribution. OCLC provides security guidance and monitoring tools to help libraries detect such abuse.

EzTix.description: EzTix is an event ticketing system.
EzTix.history: EzTix was an online event ticketing platform specializing in culinary events, festivals and tours that operated for nearly 20 years before shutting down. The original platform is no longer operational as of around 2022. A separate Caribbean-focused ticketing service operates at a similar domain but is unrelated.
EzTix.summary_abuse: EzTix was a small event ticketing platform that is now deprecated. During its operation, users reported issues with payment processing and support during the COVID-19 pandemic. No major fraud or phishing incidents documented beyond typical ticketing platform risks.

ezyVet.description: ezyVet is a cloud-based veterinary practice management platform designed to support clinical workflows, patient records, scheduling, and operational processes.
ezyVet.history: ezyVet is a cloud-based veterinary practice management system founded in New Zealand around 2010; it was acquired by Covetrus, a veterinary pharmacy and data services company, which has since been a subsidiary.
ezyVet.summary_abuse: ezyVet is a veterinary practice management platform with no notable abuse signal; it is used exclusively by licensed veterinary practices.
